With audience participation very much encouraged, this special evening of storytelling and song is not be missed.



Booking required.

specificEvents.name: The Green Knight (But it's Gay) [Ropetackle Storytellers]
specificEvents.description:
Ropetackle Storytellers hosts Niall Moorjani in an award-winning and fantastically funny retelling of the medieval classic The Green Knight, with the subtext made text! (Spoiler alert: it's gay).



 A classic Christmas game ends in Sir Gawain being peer-pressured into chopping off the Green Knight's head. Now he has to let the miraculously still-alive Green Knight return the favour. Disaster! King Arthur reckons Gawain needs to be more masculine and there's nothing manlier than a good head chopping off... so there's really no way out. Created by critically acclaimed and award-winning storyteller Niall Moorjani and co-directed with Cecily Nash. Fresh from a packed 5 star run at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival, this adaptation fabulously queers and modernizes an Arthurian classic.

specificEvents.name: Two Tales: Mumming, Puppetry & Storytelling [The Roving Jewel]
specificEvents.description:
We are pleased to welcome back The Roving Jewel after a highly popular re-telling of Sir Gawain & The Green Knight last year.



In an evolution of the Jewel, this time there will be two tales told which combine mumming, puppetry and storytelling.



This event is not aimed at children specifically but is suitable for all ages and best for ages 12+



7.00-8.30pm — £8  |  Booking required (no proof of purchase/ticket required as we have your details from purchase, just come along at the right time and date)

specificEvents.name: Cân y Dŵr - The Song of Water [Ragged Storytelling Collective]
specificEvents.showname: Cân y Dŵr - The Song of Water
specificEvents.description: The Ragged storytelling collective present our new show Cân y Dŵr - The Song of Water. Mixing folk-ritual with documentary, story with music, puppetry with poetry, Cân y Dŵr flows between mad science, mythology, folklore and history, with grief, anger, love and reverence for the waters that bring us life.

specificEvents.name: The Shahnameh — Book of Kings [Xanthe Gresham]
specificEvents.description:
Aerial artiste of the spoken word, Xanthe Gresham, dives headlong into the wild mythology of Persia, to perform sections of The Shahnameh - the epic masterpiece The Book of Kings - accompanied by the remarkable Iranian musician Arash Moradi.



Magical horses, epic heroes, and consuming passions abound in this world of romance, lust, tragedy and war. Here fathers and sons may be strangers in battle; selfish Kings move others like chess pieces across the board for their own amusement; beauty can be both a gift and a curse, and strong women break free and conquer their fate.

specificEvents.name: Bude Lit. Fest — Herd [Suited Storytellers]
specificEvents.description:
Three stories. Traditional, folk and myth.



 Stories that emerged in different places, merged and spread.



A girl and her horse. An urge to know everything. A goddess.



 Suited Storytellers take these old tales and weave them into a new fabric, exploring how you find your voice and your people, and what it takes to stay true to your inner wildness. Featuring their distinctive style and sharp suits, 'HERD' is a rich, thought-provoking and, most importantly, entertaining piece born out of the oral storytelling tradition.



 Finding our herd. Being heard. In our fast-paced, youth-obsessed modern world, these ancient stories can remind us of what really is important and that, as the years pass, 'happily ever after' may not look the way we expected.



 Duration: 55 minutes. Age Advisory: 12+ (infrequent swearing and adult themes) 



High quality food/bar/accommodation (+ Sunday Lunch!) available at The Falcon Hotel.



 Unreserved seating.



A Bude Literary Festival event.

specificEvents.name: Wild Gods & Mad Spirits [Henry Everett]
specificEvents.description:
Join storyteller and author Henry Everett in this intimate, enchanting performance exploring the tales of Pan, Robin Goodfellow & more...



This intimate spoken-word performance in the round will transport us between the mortal and faerie worlds — and explore our human connection with nature and disconnection from wildness.



Spanning tales from Ancient Greece to the realm of Faerie, join us for an enchanting evening of story in the Talliston Watchtower. Under the gaze of our own wild god, Henry will transport us with tales of some of the world's most infamous tricksters: the Great God Pan and Robin Goodfellow.

specificEvents.name: Under a Hill, a King Lays Sleeping [Bernadette Russell]
specificEvents.showname: Under a Hill, a King Lays Sleeping
specificEvents.description:
Come join local author and storyteller Bernadette Russell for a wild and magical evening of Hampshire myth and folklore - featuring the tales of the King asleep beneath a hill which is perhaps a slumbering dragon, the giant Ascapart and his battles with Bevis of Hampton, an enchanted stone circle and a ghostly horse, our local witches and their spells, incantations and potions, the Otherworld and the Bentworth Thorn, and the grampus that lives in a Yew Tree. With stories, songs, poetry and even a spell from a Hampshire Grimoire - this will be a night to remember - guaranteed fun and packed full of ancient wisdom too!



 Come and let yourself be re-enchanted by this beautiful region full of chalk downlands, heathland, forest and coastline, bustling with rich legends and fairytales. With stories, songs, and poetry this will be a night to remember- guaranteed fun and packed full of ancient wisdom too!

specificEvents.name: The Hostages of Asgard [Catherine Chadwick]
specificEvents.description: This month, Surrey Story Tellers welcomes one of its own, with Catherine Chadwick telling 'The Hostages of Asgard' - When the Aesir and Vanir gods declared peace between them after the hostilities, they exchanged hostages.  The Vanir sent Njordr and his children Freyr and Freyja to live with the Aesir in Asgard.  These three provide us with stories of an unexpected marriage, obsession with a beautiful giantess and the attainment of an incomparable golden necklace.

specificEvents.name: Tell Tales Club: Tropsyn / Morgan le Fay [Jessie Wild / Cath Little]
specificEvents.showname: Tell Tale Club: Tropsyn / Morgan le Fay
specificEvents.description:
Join local storyteller Jessie Wild as she tells a beloved Romany tale from Eastern Europe - Tropsyn. Expect adventure, jeopardy and magical transformation in the most surprising way.



 We are also excited to be welcoming the wonderful storyteller Cath Little from Cardiff who will be enchanting us with tales of Morgan Le Fey - In this masterful weaving together of ancient myths from Wales, Cath Little breathes the stories of Morgan Le Fay back to life.



 This is the first time the Tell Tales will be in the barn at The Castle Inn so Jessie thinks it is wise you bring warm clothing if it's a chilly evening and maybe a cusion for extra comfort!

specificEvents.name: Cunning Folk of the Forest & Other Tradtitional Tales [Tony 'Monty' Hirst]
specificEvents.showname: Cunning Folk of the Forest & Other Tradtitional Tales
specificEvents.description:
Making a welcome return to the Crown & Sceptre following his previous sell out show, 'The Sin Eater of Ross', storyteller Tony 'Monty' Hirst brings a new set of old tales from the Forest, as well as more traditional tales as passed down through ages.



Advance booking is encouraged, but tickets will also be available on the door.



There will still be no mention of the bears...

specificEvents.name: 1647: The Poorest Hee [Sarah Rundle]
specificEvents.description:
After five years of war, the troops of the Parliamentary Army held their leaders to account.  'What is the point of fighting the King's Tyranny, if we just end up with another set of Great Men over us?'  They wanted the vote and they wanted land. Did they get either? Come and see...



'I think that the Poorest Hee that is in England hath a life to live, as the greatest Hee; and therefore truly, Sir, I think itt clear, that every Man that is to live under a Government ought first by his own Consent to put himself under that Government...' Thomas Rainsborough, at the Putney Debates, 1647.

specificEvents.name: Fox Skin: A Dionysus Story [Amelia Ace Armande]
specificEvents.description:
On the side of a mountain called Meros a young girl is dancing. She is a sacred dancer to the Olympian gods, and life on the mountain is all she has ever known. But her story will take her far from the mountain she grew up on, and across the seas, in a dreaming, unwinding myth of the birth (and birth, and birth) of a brand new Olympian god...



Amelia Ace Armande unstoppers the origin story of Dionysus - the Greek god of wine, queer love and divine madness - with tales that are as dark, wild and heady as a sacred vintage.

specificEvents.name: Downright Rude! [TUUP, Tim Ralphs, Nell Phoenix & Sarah Liisa Wilkinson]
specificEvents.description:
Four Crick Crack Club storytellers take to the stage for a night of gloriously rude folk and fairytales. This no-holds-barred evening of innuendo and bawdiness will peep beneath the petticoats of flirtation, raise a suggestive eyebrow at forbidden love, and take the urges of men and women firmly in hand.



Lowering the tone for your delight are the glint-toothed, unprecedented preacher man TUUP, the oh-so-knowing Sarah Liisa Wilkinson, the irreverent reverend Tim Ralphs, and the naughty-but-nice Nell Phoenix.



Expect funny, saucy, and deliciously improper stories that stray below the belt.

specificEvents.name: Legends of the Cornish Landscape [Elizabeth Dale & Keith Wallis]
specificEvents.description:
Forgotten tales from Cornwall's past.



Join Keith Wallis, host of The Piskie Trap podcast, and Elizabeth Dale, writer and creator of The Cornish Bird blog for their latest talk exploring legends in the Cornish landscape. From the windswept tors and headlands, to secluded woods and valleys and rambling highways and byways, each have their tales to tell.

specificEvents.name: Another Stranger: Cressida's Tale [Stephe Harrop]
specificEvents.description:
In a world where the lives of strangers & survivors are weighed & traded for political gain, this Cressida is determined to define her value.



The wind whips her hair, as she watches the soldiers go by. And a bright face is lifted towards her, full of longing. He swears she's fair and rare as a pearl. But he doesn't know the grit within her. For Troy must fall, and Cressida — alone; among strangers - must learn the arts of survival and transformation.



To the medieval poets who shaped her legend, changeful Cressida was fickle, false, and heartless. But Cressida's Tale follows this anti-heroine's journeys from mythic Troy to England's royal court, the bitter cold of the Scottish coast, and an Elizabethan London seething with anti-migrant rage. In the process, it discovers a Cressida for our own times: a girl with a gift for resilience and reinvention.



In a world where the lives of strangers and survivors are weighed and traded for political gain, this Cressida is determined to define her own value.

specificEvents.name: Psalms from the Horse's Mouth [Hugh Lupton]
Yamanba [Laura Sampson]
specificEvents.description:
Join us on an adventure into East Asian myths and folklore  on June 4th as we join Laura Sampson on a quest to find 'Yamanba'....



  High up in the mountains, between peak and river source, squats an Old Crone, forgotten and herself forgetful ofeverything, even her name. But once, she was worthy of worship. Who is she now?\n


 'Hungry mountain hag' was the figure Laura expected to see when she first sat down in a Tokyo theatre to watch the noh Yamanba. But the being she met there was more: perplexing, awesome, enticing - built from stories not in Laura's books. Where could she find Her? 



 Fragments of Yamanba dance through East Asian myth, folklore, Buddhist parables ... and the unenlightened minds of those who dare follow them. Join Laura's climb up intrepid, intricate mountain paths, in search of a being of ravenous fury, forgotten might, and surprising compassion.

specificEvents.name: The Gods Are All Here [Phil Okwedy]
specificEvents.description:
The Gods Are All Here by Phil Okwedy



 A compelling, one-person performance storytelling piece, exploring themes of family relationships, equality, racism and freedom, through finely interwoven myth, music, personal story and folktales.



 When clearing his mother's house,storyteller Phil Okwedy made an astonishing discovery of a series of love letters from his father to his mother.Extracts from these letters form the backdrop to a powerful and compelling performance that mixes extraordinary and everyday stories of myths, folktales and song from the African diaspora with Phil's personal experiences of growing up as a child of dual heritage in 1960 & 70's Wales.



 Charting the time of life when children are said to view their parents as gods, Phil considers if his parents were, in fact, the gods he had believed them to be…The Gods Are All Here is a touching, funny and evocative performance of one man revisiting stories of his childhood alongside themes of equality, racism, freedom and justice.



 Phil Okwedy is a Welsh performance storyteller and myth-maker, born in Cardiff to a Welsh mother and Nigerian father. He draws deeply on his dual heritage and multiple cultures and performs regularly in storytelling clubs and festivals across Wales and England, as well as Internationally.

specificEvents.name: The Forest of Chance Encounters [Ropetackle Storytellers]
specificEvents.description:
'She gave me pizza with murder in her heart.'



 Four boys go into a wood, but not all of them come out again. One lad chases a hare into the forest and stumbles upon a mysterious palace with a silent, occasionally naked, heavily veiled chatelaine. The next, a cattleman, turns out to have a secret past. A third man loses his wife whilst chasing his wedding ring. And the fourth story? Well, that's a chance for the women to answer back.



 Not all the stories have happy endings: prison, pistols, poison, and skulls will feature.



 There are five narrators, two of them dead, and not every handsome hero gets his just deserts.



 The Forest of Chance Encounters is one long tale melded together from four stories in Italo Calvino's collection of Italian Folktales, masterfully told by performer storyteller Sarah Rundle.



 There are Lurve Stories and there are Anti-Lurve Stories. This show cunningly combines successful love, unsuccessful love, and Run-for-the-Hills-they're-a-Psycho love. 



 Sarah Rundle got locked in a laboratory but tunnelled her way to freedom. She ran away to drama school, and has been a storyteller for nineteen years. She has performed for the British Council, Wellcome Foundation, Geffrye Museum, Kew Gardens, the National Trust, Stockholm's Fabula Story Café, the Nivesh Festival in Delhi, Festival at the Edge, and Beyond The Border.

specificEvents.name: An Evening Of Old Songs [Soft Lap (Amy Jeffs et al.)]
specificEvents.description:
An Evening Of Old Songs Sung By 'Soft Lap'



Join us for a very special musical event with Amy Jeffs, author of Storyland and Wild, artist Gwen Burns and musician Natalie Brice to celebrate their illustrated and music-infused book, Old Songs: Stories of Love and Death from Traditional Ballads.



Doors open just before 7, kick-off is just after 7.



Bring your own refreshments.

specificEvents.name: An Evening In The Company of Amy Jeffs [Amy Jeffs, Paul Armfield]
specificEvents.showname: An Evening In The Company of Amy Jeffs
specificEvents.description:
Come and join us for an evening with best-selling author, artist, and medievalist Amy Jeffs whose books have become a genuine publishing phenomenon. We are very excited to be welcoming Amy to Medina Bookshop, her wonderful titles have been incredibly popular with our customers, reshaping how myth, history, and visual storytelling can be presented to a modern readership.



Amy will be in conversation with bookshop manager Paul Armfield.



Doors open just before 7 and we kick off just after 7, please bring your own refreshments.

specificEvents.name: Legends of the Cornish Landscape [Elizabeth Dale & Keith Wallis]
specificEvents.description:
Forgotten tales from Cornwall's past.



Join Keith Wallis, host of The Piskie Trap podcast, and Elizabeth Dale, writer and creator of The Cornish Bird blog for their latest talk exploring legends in the Cornish landscape. From the windswept tors and headlands, to secluded woods and valleys and rambling highways and byways, each have their tales to tell.

specificEvents.name: The Shahnameh [Xanthe Gresham]
specificEvents.description:
Aerial artiste of the spoken word, Xanthe Gresham, dives headlong into the wild mythology of Persia, to perform sections of The Shahnameh - the epic masterpiece The Book of Kings - accompanied by the remarkable Iranian musician Arash Moradi.



Magical horses, epic heroes, and consuming passions abound in this world of romance, lust, tragedy and war. Here fathers and sons may be strangers in battle; selfish Kings move others like chess pieces across the board for their own amusement; beauty can be both a gift and a curse, and strong women break free and conquer their fate.

specificEvents.name: Legends of the Cornish Landscape [Elizabeth Dale & Keith Wallis]
specificEvents.description:
Forgotten tales from Cornwall's past.



Join Keith Wallis, host of The Piskie Trap podcast, and Elizabeth Dale, writer and creator of The Cornish Bird blog for their latest talk exploring legends in the Cornish landscape. From the windswept tors and headlands, to secluded woods and valleys and rambling highways and byways, each have their tales to tell.

specificEvents.name: A Celebration of Crankies [Taletta Bierens, Wendy  and Michael Dacre, Sara Hurley, Peter Stevenson]
specificEvents.description:
Come for an evening of storytelling with crankies — but it's not what you think! Not grouchy characters, but magical stories in a box. Crankie storytellers are the most even-tempered people in the world (with maybe a touch of eccentricity) — and they stories they tell will give you miniature glimpses into another world. In this show you will discover the art of the crankie: a story told through scrolling pictures in a box turned by 'cranks' - a delightful and unexpected folk artform which brings a whole new dimension to storytelling for all ages.



In this show you will discover a whole new world of unforgettably thrilling and atmospheric stories: and meet some of the most talented crankie performers from across the country and beyond. Encounter the Snake on the Western Wave told and sung by Wendy Dacre, shadow puppeteer and textile artist extraordinaire: and Sarah Hurley's encounters with the more than human, in spooky tales from her Devon roots. Meet illustrator-author Peter Stevenson, launching three books of Welsh folktales, in magnificent crankie form: and all the way from the extraordinary bee garden in Bloemendahl, Netherlands, storyteller Taletta Bierens with the story of the Flying Ship.

specificEvents.name: Fringe Theatrefest — Herd [Suited Storytellers]
specificEvents.description:
Three stories. Traditional, folk and myth.



 Stories that emerged in different places, merged and spread.



A girl and her horse. An urge to know everything. A goddess.



 Suited Storytellers take these old tales and weave them into a new fabric, exploring how you find your voice and your people, and what it takes to stay true to your inner wildness. Featuring their distinctive style and sharp suits, 'HERD' is a rich, thought-provoking and, most importantly, entertaining piece born out of the oral storytelling tradition.



 Finding our herd. Being heard. In our fast-paced, youth-obsessed modern world, these ancient stories can remind us of what really is important and that, as the years pass, 'happily ever after' may not look the way we expected.



 Duration: 55 minutes. Age Advisory: 12+ (infrequent swearing and adult themes) 



A Barnstaple Fringe Theatrefest event.

specificEvents.name: The Fern Flower [Jane & Guy Lambourne]
Yde and Olive: A Medieval Love Story [Daisy Black]
specificEvents.showname: Yde and Olive: A Medieval Love Story
specificEvents.description:
This month (26 June at 7.30pm) we welcome storyteller and academic Daisy Black to Three Heads in a Well Storytelling Club. Daisy will be telling the remarkable thirteenth-century tale of Yde, who dresses herself as a knight and cuts her own paths to freedom. A tale of disguise adventure, gender fluidity, straight-talking angels, and the power of queer love.



 Weaving medieval narrative with modern folk song, this performance will take you from birthing chambers to military barracks, through moonlit country lanes and woods stained with slaughter, and from quiet chapels to court chambers crackling with gossip.



This month we are in-person only.

specificEvents.name: Guid Crack: Midsummer Dreaming [Harriet Grindley]
specificEvents.description:
Dreams pursued, wishes granted or nightmares uncovered, how far would you go to make your dreams come true? Angus Òg, Lord of Summer, bringer of dreams, is at the height of his powers in this Midsummer season. Stories, songs, riddles, spells in the realm of the 'what if' to enchant the long summer evening.



Another fun and friendly session of Edinburgh’s monthly storytelling club held upstairs in the Waverley Bar, with the usual open-floor section for anyone to share a story, song or poem around the theme or otherwise.

specificEvents.name: Secret Tales From Wales [Daniel Morden]
specificEvents.description:
Join us for an unforgettable evening as award-winning storyteller Daniel Morden shares SECRET TALES FROM WALES.



Gather close and be transported into the rich world of traditional Welsh stories. These are stories once told by hearth and candle - passed from voice to voice, shaped by laughter, silence, and the darkness outside. You'll hear tales of bravery and trickery, kindness and cruelty, enchantment and escape. No screens. No hurry. Just stories, told the old way!

specificEvents.name: Welsh Whisperers: Stories of Barmouth
specificEvents.description:
Stories from Barmouth and Wales for adults.



Join the Dragon Storytellers and listen to stories from Barmouth, Wales and beyond. You'll hear traditional folk tales, myths and local legends and get to know more about our beautiful country and the people and creatures that live there.



These stories are meant for an adult audience

specificEvents.name: I Will Survive [Elizabeth Dale & Keith Wallis]
specificEvents.description:
I Will Survive is a comedy storytelling show about grief; more specifically, the complicated experience of losing a parent you didn't really like.



Blending storytelling, humour, and slightly unhinged academic energy (yes, there's a PowerPoint), Hari Berrow explores the messy, contradictory feelings that can come with loss — and questions the idea that there's a 'right' way to grieve.



Doors open from 6.15pm. Coffee shop open before the show. Start time 7pm. Half of ticket sales are fundraising for the centre.

specificEvents.name: Full Moon Story Walk [Sue Bailey]
specificEvents.description:
Enjoy a sunset walk around the top of Culver Down, the battery and Bembridge fort with stops along the way for moon stories – some true, some not so true.  Hopefully we will see the full moon rise over the sea about 9pm.



There are some stiles, steep sections, and the terrain can be uneven and muddy after rain.

specificEvents.name: Tell Tales Club: Sheherazade's Stories [Jessie Wild]
Open Tellers' Special Night [feat. Steve Dimmer]
specificEvents.showname: Open Tellers' Special Night [feat. Steve Dimmer]

specificEvents.name: The Cauldron of Plenty: A Feast of Midsummer Tales
specificEvents.showname: The Cauldron of Plenty: A Feast of Midsummer Tales

specificEvents.name: We Need to Talk About Cuchulain [Lara McClure]
specificEvents.showname: We Need to Talk About Cuchulain
specificEvents.description:
It's time to meet Cuchulain but from a completely new persective. We know him as an Irish warrior hero and demigod but he was also; husband, father, lover and guard dog. In Iron Age Ulster, stories came out of the mouths of bards and went into the ears of everyone else. Lara McClure's darkly amusing and assuredly gnarly yarn approaches archetypal hero Cuchulain through the testimony of a trio of Warrior Women - his sworn enemy Queen Medb, his ardent admirer the Goddess of War, and of course his doting Nana. 



rSuitable for age 14+.



We will be live and in the room and also streaming via Zoom. We're loving our international reach and enjoy the 'shout outs' to our global audience. So, if you're willing and able, schmooze on down to the Imperial Rooms in Matlock and join us live. If you're elsewhere in the world or can't join us live for whatever reason then kick back, feet up and click on the link.



We are delighted to tell you that soup, cakes, teas coffees etc are available from our kitchen.  We do still operate with good old fashioned cash, kerching! so bring your purse to cross our palms with silver. All monies from the kitchen help to fund our ongoing story nights. You are still most welcome to bring a bottle of something cheeky with you.



We urge you to book in advance, if you are able as tickets often sell out. 



To help our more technically challenged friends we have a very small number of tickets available on the door. We suggest you arrive early if you wish to secure one of these pay in person tickets. 



We at Matlock Storytelling Cafe are embracing our quest; to bring top quality performances to adult audiences.



Grab a ticket, turn off your phone and join us on a journey. Online ticket sales will close at 7pm on the night.



The doors opens at 7.00pm, stories and music  7.30-10

specificEvents.name: Under the Tree — A Storytelling Evening [Island Storytellers]
specificEvents.showname: Under the Tree — A Storytelling Evening
specificEvents.description:
Join the Island Storytellers in Shanklin as they take you on storytelling journeys that begin under one of Rylstone Gardens' magnificent trees.



Spend the evening enjoying a brilliant medley of folk tales about trees. The stories may branch out to many times and many countries, but all will have their roots deep in the ground, connecting nature and humanity.



Bring a chair, a picnic and dress for the weather. Meet near the bandstand.



Free event - donations asked for St. Catherine's School.



Stories aimed at adults but children aged 10+ welcome.



Limited parking nearby, accessible via no. 2 bus route.



No booking required.



Part of the Isle of Wight Biosphere Festival.



Email theislandstorytellers@gmail.com for more info.

specificEvents.name: Stories for Midsummer [Maria Credali]
specificEvents.description:
Listen... as Midsummer traditions and folklore come to life: outwitting the devil, becoming a hare, and braving the standing stones...



Stories for Midsummer - Folklore and fairytale for adult audiences.



Listen…as Midsummer traditions, folklore and fairytale come to life.



A clever maiden outwits the Devil himself.



A girl transforms into a hare.



And what really happened at the standing stones?



The show will also feature an original story which was commissioned and performed for The Royal Shakespeare Company by Maria.



Perfect for lovers of oral storytelling, and those fascinated by folklore and old magic, this is the perfect way to celebrate the magic, mystery, and mischief of Midsummer.

specificEvents.name: Downright Rude! [TUUP, Tim Ralphs, Nell Phoenix & Sarah Liisa Wilkinson]
specificEvents.description:
Four Crick Crack Club storytellers take to the stage for a night of gloriously rude folk and fairytales. This no-holds-barred evening of innuendo and bawdiness will peep beneath the petticoats of flirtation, raise a suggestive eyebrow at forbidden love, and take the urges of men and women firmly in hand.



Lowering the tone for your delight are the glint-toothed, unprecedented preacher man TUUP, the oh-so-knowing Sarah Liisa Wilkinson, the irreverent reverend Tim Ralphs, and the naughty-but-nice Nell Phoenix.



Expect funny, saucy, and deliciously improper stories that stray below the belt.

specificEvents.name: Lamentable Tales, Merry Gestes and Pleasant Histories [Dave Tonge]
specificEvents.showname: Lamentable Tales, Merry Gestes and Pleasant Histories
specificEvents.description:
Join storyteller and historian Dave Tonge and hear the tales that filled Johnson's London, from ballads and chapbooks to legends shared in coffee houses and market places.



In his Dictionary of the English Language, Dr Samuel Johnson defines a story as 'A petty fiction', and 'An idle, or trifling tale'. But whatever the definition there was an explosion of stories finding their way into print in his life time. 



 While some printers were producing learned works like Dr Johnson's Dictionary, many a back street press was knocking out broadside ballads, almanacs, chapbooks and other crudely produced cheap print. 



 Reworking's of much older tales like Jack the Giant Killer, Aesops Fables and all new versions of the Merry Gestes of Robin Hood, to tales focused on England's capital city. Stories like A Pleasant History of Taffy's  Progress to London and The Blind Beggar of Bethnal Green. Stories that were popular among polite society and the common folk alike, and shared in the coffee houses, market places, the back street alehouses, and wherever 18th-century people gathered together. 



 Dave Tonge is a storyteller and historian. He is the author of three books, Tudor Folk Tales, Norfolk Folk Tales for Children, and Medieval Tales for Children which is a fusion of medieval folktales and horrible history style anecdotes. He has been storytelling for 25 years and conducted sessions for the Ashmolean and British Museum, as well as for English Heritage, National Trust and the Shakespeare's Birthplace Trust

specificEvents.name: Tales of Robin Hood [Ropetackle Storytellers]
specificEvents.description:
There's singing in the Greenwood, news of an archery competition, and someone keeps foiling the Sheriff of Nottingham's plans – it can only be Robin Hood! Ropetackle Storytellers gathers their own band of Merry Men to share some of Robin's best tales, through songs and stories. Hear how Robin first became an outlaw, how he met the likes of Friar Tuck, Will Scarlet and Little John, and exactly what happened in that infamous archery competition!



These tub-thumping tales of rebellion and raucous adventure are brought to you by Ace & Joshua, the Ropetackle Storytellers club founders, along with Heidi McNie (Sing a Song of Sussex), Marina Evans (To Shame the Devil), Jon Norman-Mason (You're Dead to Me, Beowulf), Sophie Gibson (The Wasteland Wanderer), Abbie Simmonds (Strange Times Storytelling) and Joanna Gilar (Storycommons CIC)

specificEvents.name: Women Who Bore Through Walls [Sarah Rundle]
specificEvents.description:
What happens when your husband locks you up and it's the 16 th century and there’s no Hooman Rights? You burrow, that's what.



Stories of women, from Italy, Morocco, and England, unjustly imprisoned by jealous spouses, all using their wit and grit to survive, escape, and thrive. With tips on tunnelling, staying sane, and where to hide your jewellery in an emergency.



Sarah Rundle got locked in a laboratory but tunnelled her way to freedom. She ran away to drama school, and for the last eighteen years has been a professional storyteller. She has devised works for the British Council, Wellcome Foundation, Geffrye Museum, Kew Gardens, and the National Trust, and performed at Stockholm's Fabula Story Café, the Nivesh Festival in Delhi, Kolkata Literature Festival, Nottingham’s Theatre Royal, and Festival at the Edge.



Also featuring tales from your hosts, music, fun and frolics.

specificEvents.name: Wild Man [Martin Maudsley]
specificEvents.description:
Wild Man by Martin Maudsley



 Martin Maudsley offers a double-bill of stirring stories rooted in North European mythology and percolated through the rich imagery of oral storytelling. From the deep, dark woods of Grimms' tales to the cold, airy mountains of Norse legend: two tales of two young men on two twisting journeys through the wild places of the world to face their fate — and find the wildness within themselves… A carefully crafted performance of spoken words and vivid images, exploring adolescence, desire and destiny, as well as our deep-rooted relationship with the natural world.



 “The themes of flight, freedom and a sense of the wild resonate strongly in this fine retelling where confinement, and perhaps even civilisation, mean death.” Bristol Evening Post.



 After a previous career in environmental science, Martin Maudsley has been a professional storyteller for over twenty years. Based in rural Dorset, he works widely in schools, festivals and theatres offering fresh retellings of traditional tales, as well as creative storytelling projects for national organisations. He has recently toured as a professional storyteller in China, South Korea and Taiwan. Martin has a particular passion for stories that connect people with place and the natural world, and until recently was storyteller-in-residence for environmental arts charity Common Ground. His first book Telling the Seasons was published in 2022 and his children's folklore book Monsterlogica is out later this year.



 This is the second of a series of Wild Stories events on Dartmoor in the summer of 2026: we aim to showcase amazing storytelling in extraordinary places. It is hosted jointly by Dartmoor Storytellers and Moor Barton Wilding.



 Age suitability 16+. No dogs please. If you need to bring an assistance dog please let us know beforehand.



 This event will be in the woods, held outdoors around the fire under the shelter of White Deer Lodge at Moor Barton. Come prepared for the outdoors with suitable clothing and footwear. Refreshments will be available, and there are compost toilets. You can park nearby; there is a short walk to the venue, with an earth path which can be uneven underfoot.



 Details of how to get to the site, and what to bring, will be sent through to all ticket holders near to the date of the event.



 Moor Barton Wilding CIC is a 120 acre project on the north east shoulder of Dartmoor dedicated to rewilding land and people. The land is made up of a mosaic of ecologically rich habitats - including wood pasture, hazel coppice, transitional edge and wildflower glades, closed canopy woodland, wetland, veteran trees and conifer woodland - and is a key site on Dartmoor for species reintroduction. Believing that humans are part of nature with a key role to play in restoring our ecological systems, Moor Barton engages communities near and far, offering many opportunities to learn about and get involved in different aspects of rewilding. https://www.moorbarton.org/

specificEvents.name: Secrets, Stories and Songs from the North East of Scotland [Sheeha Blackhall & Pauline Cordiner]
specificEvents.showname: Secrets, Stories and Songs from the North East of Scotland
specificEvents.description:
Join renowned North-East writer, poet, singer and storyteller Sheena Blackhall with storyteller Pauline Cordiner for an evening of folklore, hidden history, ghostly tales and traditional songs.



Held in the beautiful surroundings of historic St Mary's Chapel, this adults-only event celebrates the stories, traditions and rich Doric and Scots heritage of the North East.

specificEvents.name: Beowulf Storytelling Around the Fire [Lizzie Bryant]
specificEvents.description:
Experience storytelling Saxon-style around an open fire with a performance of Beowulf, the oldest story in the English language.



 Back by popular demand after three sell-out performances, storyteller Lizzie Bryant returns for another dynamic and engaging retelling of Beowulf.



 Experience storytelling Saxon-style with a performance of Beowulf, the oldest story in the English language. Gather around the fire, step back into a Saxon world and experience the enduring tale of the Beowulf, hero of men and the monsters that brought him fame. It's a tale of blood feuds and fury that's been told for a thousand years or more.



 Lizzie's gripping performance of this epic old English poem is presented in (mostly!) modern English prose in two equal parts with an interval (a glass of local apple juice included!) and a Q&A afterwards.

specificEvents.name: VFringe: Tales from the Isle of Wight Ghost Experience [Mark Tuckey]
specificEvents.showname: Tales from the Isle of Wight Ghost Experience
specificEvents.description:
Marc Tuckey brings his famous Ghost Experience to Ventnor Fringe!



 For over 30 years, Marc has been enthralling both visitors to and residents of the Isle of Wight with haunting historical tales of grisly ghosts, ghouls and ghastly goings-on. Many have enjoyed the walks he leads with his team every summer season. If you don't feel like taking a walk though, now is your chance to settle back in your seat for some more spooky stories – this time with a family feel.



 Want to know what connects Shanklin to Jekyll and Hyde? And Jack the Ripper? Ever wondered why some places on the Island just have an atmosphere? What was seen in the well at Carisbrooke Castle? What happened when the French invaded? And just why was a tomb in Arreton Church defaced – and by whom? Why are there always three lighthouse keepers, never just two? Then there are the Arreton monks who can only be seen from the knees upwards…. And the Island's very own ghost ship….



 Whether you're interested in the Island's past; love a silly spectral tale; like a laugh or just enjoy a well-told story, you can expect a mix of little known facts from the past and some hilarious and haunting tales you won't forget.



 This show is produced with a family audience in mind, so you can be sure there will be fun and laughter and nothing too scary….. so come along and be entertained by the master story teller (and his companions).

specificEvents.name: VFringe: Medieval Medley [Island Storytellers]
specificEvents.description:
Step back in time for an evening of Medieval mischief, mystery and mayhem!



These timeless tales feature a cast of truly unforgettable characters. A gang of youths embark on a pursuit to hunt down death, a clueless carpenter braces for a biblical flood and a swindler swears he has the skill of turning sticks into silver.



So join the Island Storytellers for an evening of warmth, wisdom and wit in this fun and lively celebration of community storytelling.

specificEvents.name: VFringe: Solent Tales, Macabre Stories [Monty Storyteller]
specificEvents.description:
It's a time for spying, and a time for sabotage.



The last quarter of the 18th century and Britain is at war with France and America. Portsmouth, as the home of the Navy, is a target... 



So come here tell, of John the Painter, incendiary and arsonist, hanged, on The Hard, from the highest gallows to be used in England. And of David Tyrie, whose particularly gruesome execution on Southsea Common pulled in a crowd to rival that of the Victorious Festival today. And of... well, that would be telling...



Taking time out from tale telling alongside some of the island's finest musicians, traditional storyteller Tony 'Monty' Hirst brings a solo show of historical tales to Ventnor Fringe. Carefully researched from contemporary newspaper sources of the time, which will be surely made available as storynotes merch, Monty has read them all and will tell you what he remembers. Because it's the story that's left when the facts have been forgotten.

specificEvents.name: VFringe: Tarot Tales [Merl Fluin]
specificEvents.description:
Delve deep into the wisdom and wonder of Tarot with this wildly imaginative live storytelling show.



For hundreds of years, Tarot cards have fascinated and beguiled. They've told fortunes, uncovered secrets, and held up a dark mirror to the mysteries of life.



Now performance storyteller Merl Fluin reveals the Major Arcana in a whole new light. Combining the Tarot's symbolism with traditional tales from myth and folklore, she'll lead you on a dance where every card is a portal to a glittering storyworld. Prepare to be spellbound.

specificEvents.name: VFringe: Faery Tale Blues — Songs'n'Stories [Monty + The AnyWhens]
specificEvents.description:
Following a rapidly sold out event at Ventnor Fringe last year ('Protest Days, Rebellion Nights'), storyteller Tony 'Monty' Hirst and and folk/blues duo The AnyWhens (Glenn Koppany and Sue Cain) bring their new show 'Faery Tale Blues' to the Bijou Tent for 2026.



Featuring original and traditional songs, and traditional and historical tales, Monty + The AnyWhens invite you take a step outside the ordinary, to another world that is forever hidden in plain sight, to the lift the veil to the other world, the land of the Fae, and the time of Once.



 Bio: based on the Isle of Wight, Monty + The AnyWhens, aka Tony 'Monty' Hirst and folk duo The AnyWhens (Glenn Koppany and Sue Cain) combine songs, tunes and stories to transport audiences to other times and other places, recognisable, yet otherworldly.



Regularly performing 'turn taking' sets around the Island, their first show, 'Protest Days, Rebellion Nights: Songs and the Stories of the Luddite' rapidly sold out at Ventnor Fringe in 2025, as well as other venues on both the Island and the mainland.

specificEvents.name: Deliver Us From Evil [Aaron Oliver]
specificEvents.description:
Surrey Story Tellers' last summer evening of storytelling at Three Heads in a Well this year is on Friday 24 July (at 7.30pm) and sees Aaron Oliver telling 'Deliver Us From Evil' with music from Diana Redgrave.



The forgotten story of the first recorded case of Tourette's Syndrome: a 15th Century priest who believed he was possessed by a demon.Aaron Oliver dives into the mind of a religious leader at war with his own body and asks: how do the stories we tell ourselves change us?



Aaron Oliver's work explores themes of queerness, disability, humanity and hope, taking inspiration from traditional folktales, myths and legends, as well as diving into forgotten true stories from the past.



Diana Redgrave is a musician based in Kent and London. She plays the cello, shruti box, tongue drums and a variety of other sound-makers to evoke atmospheres in stories

specificEvents.name: Stories from Staffordshire [Janet Crouch]
specificEvents.description:
The tales in this show all have a link with Staffordshire. Some will be based on actual historical events. Local legends and myths form a significant part. There will certainly be a ghost story. The stories may be amusing, comical perhaps ridiculous.

Rating: 12+

specificEvents.name: VFringe: Birds of a Feather [Sue Bailey]
specificEvents.description:
As massive as an eagle, as tiny as a lark, as colourful as a kingfisher and as plain as a sparrow, feathery tales from the folklore of the land.



Stories as diverse and widespread as they are numerous.



In between tales there will be fun and mayhem : Can you tell your cuckoo from your curlew? Your blackbird from your buzzard? Whose bird call is that? What is a bird in the hand worth? And what does the early bird catch?



Isle of Wight storyteller Sue Bailey tells stories which definitely wouldn't share the same nest.

specificEvents.name: VFringe: The Fabled Isle [Holly Medland]
specificEvents.description:
Discover the Isle of Wight's strange history through its stories, where fact and folklore weave seamlessly together.



With an array of characters featuring sly smugglers, heroic hermits and raging river spirits, each tale holds at least a touch of truth - though exactly how much is for you to decide.



Following sell-out shows in 2024 and 2025, Holly Medland returns to Ventnor Fringe with an enchanting retelling of the traditional stories that have shaped the Island and its people.

specificEvents.name: Folktales and Myths of North Wales [Dragon Storytellers]
specificEvents.showname: Folktales and Myths of North Wales
specificEvents.description:
Come and listen to the stories of these mountains, lakes, fields and rivers and all that used to live there - and some still do!



The Dragon Storytellers will tell traditional stories about kings and queens, mystical beasts, otherworldly creatures and magical goings-on.



Stories will be told in English with a smattering of Welsh. They are intended for an adult audience.

specificEvents.name: Hot Crones [Debs Newbold]
specificEvents.description:
SUITABLE FOR: adults 18+, with Parental Guidance, 16+



CONTENT: This performance contains allusions to sex, gynaecology, seduction, and some more sex.

specificEvents.name: TBA [Tim Ralphs]
Beowulf [Hugh Lupton]
specificEvents.description:
Beowulf by Hugh Lupton



 Beowulf is the oldest story in the English language — and it still has the power to raise the hairs on the back of the neck! It tells of a hero's life, a life that is mapped by three tremendous blood-curdling encounters.



 As a young man Beowulf defeats the monster Grendel and his even more terrifying Mother, the original creatures from the Black Lagoon. Then, as an old man, he tries his strength against a gold guarding Fire-Drake (a dragon). Alongside these adrenalin-charged encounters the story explores the journey we all make from the seeming invincibility of youth to the heroic vulnerability of old age.



 The story has had a tremendous effect on the imagination of many writers and poets, most notably J.R.R. Tolkien. Without Beowulf there would have been no Lord of the Rings.



 Hugh Lupton is an award-winning storyteller, performer and author. He is also a poet and lyricist. His work spans over 35 years, working on both solo projects and in partnership with other artists, writers, musicians, illustrators and performers. He has toured both nationally and internationally and has performed at the RSC, the National Theatre and the Barbican, as well as at schools, community venues and arts centres the length and breadth of Britain. His repertoire ranges from Greek epic to Grimms Fairy Tales, from Norse and Celtic myth to East Anglian folk-tales, from the Great War to John Clare. Thematically his work often explores our place in the landscape and the deep cultural memories we carry. Hugh is based in Norfolk.



 This is the third of a series of Wild Stories events on Dartmoor in the summer of 2026: we aim to showcase amazing storytelling in extraordinary places. It is hosted jointly by Dartmoor Storytellers and Moor Barton Wilding.



 Age suitability 16+. No dogs please. If you need to bring an assistance dog please let us know beforehand.



 This event will be in the woods, held outdoors around the fire under the shelter of White Deer Lodge at Moor Barton. Come prepared for the outdoors with suitable clothing and footwear. Refreshments will be available, and there are compost toilets. You can park nearby; there is a short walk to the venue, with an earth path which can be uneven underfoot.



 Details of how to get to the site, and what to bring, will be sent through to all ticket holders near to the date of the event.



 Moor Barton Wilding CIC is a 120 acre project on the north east shoulder of Dartmoor dedicated to rewilding land and people. The land is made up of a mosaic of ecologically rich habitats - including wood pasture, hazel coppice, transitional edge and wildflower glades, closed canopy woodland, wetland, veteran trees and conifer woodland - and is a key site on Dartmoor for species reintroduction. Believing that humans are part of nature with a key role to play in restoring our ecological systems, Moor Barton engages communities near and far, offering many opportunities to learn about and get involved in different aspects of rewilding. https://www.moorbarton.org/

specificEvents.name: We All Came By Boat [Clare Murphy]
specificEvents.description:
Myths. Mischief. Migration. The new show from storyteller and guest on The Blindboy Podcast, Clare Murphy (The Spanking Goddess & Other Discarded Tales, The Liar King)



At the edge of Europe sit a bunch of islands, forming a chaotic stone path out into the vast Atlantic ocean. These islands have always been inhabited by those who came by boat.



We arrived as seekers, invaders, siblings, refugees and even lovers. We fulfilled prophecies, dodged floods, escaped forced marriages, and pulled our fate from the stars.



A wild night of storytelling fusing myth, geology and human migration.

specificEvents.name: Doom Scrolls — The Never Ending Prophecies of the Sibyls [Lucy Lill]
specificEvents.showname: Doom Scrolls — The Never Ending Prophecies of the Sibyls
specificEvents.description:
Possessed by gods?



Off their heads on laughing gas?



The mouthpieces of the state?



Or the ancient world's most influential influencers?



The prophecies of the Sibyls thread through ancient myth. Feared, revered and frequently misunderstood, the Sibyls stood at the crossroads of religion, politics and performance. Their visions promised answers while leaving everyone wondering what they actually meant.



From a mountain in the southeast of Italy to the depths of the underworld, Lucy Lill tells the stories of the women whose words founded cities, brought down armies and caused an epic amount of confusion.



She might know your future, but that doesn’t mean she’s going to tell you...

specificEvents.name: The Saga of the Ring [Jon Mason]
specificEvents.description: Sigurd the dragonslayer, Brunhild the valkyrie, Gudrun the princess, and a treasure that brings doom to mortals and gods alike...

specificEvents.name: Open Tellers' Special Night [feat. Lynette Hill]
specificEvents.showname: Open Tellers' Special Night [feat. Lynette Hill]

specificEvents.name: The Girl Who Didn't Die [Emily Hennessey + Sheema Mukherjee]
specificEvents.description:
Born a princess. Raised a prince. Reborn a warrior.



Making a pact with the God of Destruction is always going to have consequences. But in a world of cosmic matchmakers, divine curses and secrets set to explode, every arrow must eventually reach its target.



One of the most radical figures of the Indian epic, the Mahabharata, Shikandi’s boundary-shattering story follows the journey of a soul who battled across lifetimes to settle old scores, dancing the karmic tightrope while playing with fire.



With music by maverick sitar player, Sheema Mukherjee, this is a pulse-pounding, hair-raising, heart-lifting mythological rollercoaster, soaring between the worlds of gods and mortals to break the rules and defy destiny.

specificEvents.name: Mama, Me and ... [Baden Prince]
HOW (NOT) TO DIE long ago in Finland [Sarah Liisa Wilkinson]
specificEvents.showname: HOW (NOT) TO DIE long ago in Finland
specificEvents.description:
By the edge of a deep forest and the shore of a biting sea, an old man sets his mind on a journey to the underworld - to learn the secrets of the dead. But – and this is where it gets tricky – he wants to stay alive.



Sarah Liisa Wilkinson brings you tales from the twisting roots of Finnish myth and folklore: a woman spinning her own iron hair; a shapeshifting sage; a giant, fat with spells, sleeping deep in the earth; a coat of fire, gloves of pain — dangerous tools and hard lessons on how to live, how to let go... and how to come back.



Duration: 75mins (TBC)



Content: This performance contains references to death, threat, and violence contextualised within a paradigm of mythic narrative, archetype, and metaphor.



Suitable for: Adults 14+
