daniel-morden.name: Daniel Morden
daniel-morden.bio: Daniel Morden has been a professional storyteller since 1989. He has travelled the world, collecting and telling traditional stories. He has a huge repertoire, from timeless Greek myths and thrilling adventures to awful jokes. He has given the Opening Address at Sydney Writers Festival, won awards for his books and was recently awarded The Hay Festival Medal.

the-travelling-talesman-cliff-eastabrook.name: The Travelling Talesman (Cliff Eastabrook)
the-travelling-talesman-cliff-eastabrook.bio:
Cliff Eastabrook, aka 'The Travelling Talesmand', is spoken word narrative performer of myths, legends, folktales and the occasional poem, a storyteller! His energetic and engaging performance is adapted on a per gig basis for a wide variety of adult and family audiences. He has over twenty years professional experience, is a member of Equity and can provide risk assessments on request.



 He has toured the country from Penzance to York, for feasts, festivals and fun since the early nineties with tales of Norse Gods, Celtic mysteries, clever girls and dragon Slayers, medieval mayhem, giants, goblins and halloween horrors. He originally specialised in Northern European tales but stories are now drawn from all over the world. He is constantly expanding his repertoire, reading myths from various cultures, chasing down unusual variations on folk tales and researching, learning or creating new adventures for special theme or site specific events.

alys-torrance.name: Alys Torrance
alys-torrance.bio:
Alys is a writer, storyteller and actor.



Alys also runs workshops and courses from time to time.



She's most often found doing comedy, clowning, and improvising, appearing on, and writing for, the stage, TV, and the radio. She tells stories in clubs, schools, at festivals and in theatres. She also runs Story Jam, a monthly storytelling night. Sometimes she is serious.

nell-phoenix.name: Nell Phoenix
nell-phoenix.bio:
Nell has been performing professionally since graduating from Goldsmiths College. She has performed with theatre companies for 12 years touring all around the world - from the Blackpool Grand to the Sydney Opera House; all over the UK, Ireland, Eastern and Western Europe, Australia and in all sorts of spaces and places in-between.



She is Artistic Director of StoryNight at Torriano, Kentish Town's thriving performance storytelling clubs for adults. Nell is a passionate promoter of storytelling as a contemporary performance art.

nick-hennessey.name: Nick Hennessey
nick-hennessey.bio:
Nick Hennessey is an internationally acclaimed storyteller, playwright, folk singer, song writer and musician. He has told stories for more than 20 years, performing at venues such as the South Bank Centre and Royal Albert Hall. He is particularly drawn to North European cultural traditions and has been awarded high honours in Finland for his work on their National Epic, Kalevala.



 He is a sought after artistic collaborator, working with top of the field musicians and storytellers. He has broadcast documentaries for BBC Radio 4 and his musical work has featured on BBC Radio 3's Late Junction. With a deep appreciation for the key role that stories play in how we learn about and express ourselves and the world, whether in businesses or schools, he works extensively in helping draw out our natural storytelling skills.

island-storytellers.name: Island Storytellers
island-storytellers.bio: Informal story telling group on the Isle of Wight.

cath-little.name: Cath Little
cath-little.bio:
Cardiff Storyteller and Singer Cath Little has 'rough magic' in her voice, and in her words 'the gift of the story comes through.' She has a strong belief in the power of stories to connect us to one another, to the land, and to the people who once lived here.



 She tells traditional stories from her Irish English heritage and her Welsh homeland. Cath enjoys re-imagining and retelling stories from The Mabinogion.



 Cath helps run the Cardiff Storytelling Circle and curates their seasonal concerts, Tales for the Turning Year. She tells and listens to stories at Oasis, a Cardiff Charity which offers a warm Welsh welcome to refugees and asylum seekers.

lizzie-bryant.name: Lizzie Bryant
lizzie-bryant.bio:
Lizzie Bryant is Dorset based oral storyteller and writer, creating and delivering storytelling performances, story walks, bespoke workshops and training, working and playing with stories and the imagination to inspire connection, community, and creativity. After many years in the film industry, Lizzie retrained as a stonemason and with her hammer and chisel, shaped stone. Her work on crumbling churches led her into the wilder places to explore the old stories, myths and legends told by our ancestors and held in the land and she tumbled into the world of storytelling.



Lizzie now draws upon the treasure trove of world myths and legends and a word hoard of traditional tales, wonder tales and folk lore. She has created a number of site specific work including Pixies and Pillywiggins of Parley Wood, interactive adventure story walks in West Parley Wood for Sting in the Tale Storytelling Festival; Speaking Bones, Dancing Stones, an original bronze age family story walk around Avebury stone circle commissioned by National Trust; Dragon's Hope, an story for Hallr Wood commissioned by Summerlands Storytelling Festival. She has been commissioned and performed original stories for Salisbury Cathedral's Craftivate festival. With an MA in creative writing for children from Winchester University, Lizzie regularly writes children's stories for Serial Mash, an online literacy resource for schools.

sef-townsend.name: Sef Townsend
sef-townsend.bio:
Sef Townsend draws on his personal experience of living and working on all five continents; and on reminiscence and language support work in immigrant and refugee communities to inform his workshops in participatory storytelling and music.



 He has been running Arts projects with people in exile and immigrant communities since 1995. Through storytelling, song and 'join-in' music games he emphasises the use of traditional material from the participants' original backgrounds, and promote work that enables other cultures to be seen as an invaluable resource contributing to a shared diversity in the wider community.

bronia-evers.name: Bronia Evers
bronia-evers.bio:
Bronia is a storyteller, designer, maker and puppeteer.



Her practice combines spoken word storytelling with hand crafted performing objects, sets and puppets. Her current work focusses on creating moving panoramas - a pre-cinematic form of moving image (also known as crankies), and embedding these in live and digital performances.

cath-little-bronia-evers.name: Cath Little | Bronia Evers
cath-little-bronia-evers.bio:
Cardiff Storyteller and Singer Cath Little has 'rough magic' in her voice, and in her words 'the gift of the story comes through.' She has a strong belief in the power of stories to connect us to one another, to the land, and to the people who once lived here.



Bronia is a storyteller, designer, maker and puppeteer. Her practice combines spoken word storytelling with hand crafted performing objects, sets and puppets. Her current work focusses on creating moving panoramas - a pre-cinematic form of moving image (also known as crankies), and embedding these in live and digital performances.

nick-hennessey-emily-hennessey.name: Nick  Hennessey | Emily Hennessey
nick-hennessey-emily-hennessey.bio:
Husband and wife team, Nick and Emily Hennessey are both acclaimed storytellers in their own right.



Nick Hennessey is an internationally acclaimed storyteller, playwright, folk singer, song writer and musician. He has told stories for more than 20 years, performing at venues such as the South Bank Centre and Royal Albert Hall. He is particularly drawn to North European cultural traditions and has been awarded high honours in Finland for his work on their National Epic, Kalevala.



Emily Hennessey is a playful and dynamic performance storyteller with a great love of Hindu mythology. She has travelled over 10,000 miles across India and Nepal by train, bus, rattling rickshaw and bicycle. Emily has toured in India, Iceland and Japan and performed at venues and festivals across Europe. She spent many months at the Kattaikkuttu School in Tamil Nadu, learning from the children who perform stories from the Mahabharata through music, dance and song.

am-hellberg-moberg.name: Anna Maria Vilhelmina Hellberg Moberg
am-hellberg-moberg.bio:
UK-based, Swedish author, journalist and storyteller Anna Maria Vilhelmina Hellberg Moberg (formerly Anna Maria Espsäter). She is the author of 25+ books, including five works of fiction, numerous travel books, three books about cats and, most recently, a book of Swedish folk tales (published October 2024 by the History Press).



 In recent years, as Vilhelmina Hellberg Moberg, she has branched out into live storytelling for adults. She is multilingual, both as a writer and speaker/storyteller, and a keen photographer who's had several solo exhibitions in the UK and Sweden.

riika-polonen.name: Riika Polonen
riika-polonen.bio:
As a child Riika discovered that storybooks could transport her to other worlds of fairytale, myth and magic. To this day she still enjoys visiting those faraway places, and as a storyteller she is able to take others with her on that journey.



For over 15 years Riika has been performing as a storyteller in Finland and in recent years in venues across the UK from Oxford to Orkney, and internationally from Morocco to Lapland. 



Riika also works in collaberation with her husband, folk musician Richard Clarke to create their very popular song and story performances.

niall-moorjani.name: Niall Moorjani
niall-moorjani.bio: Niall Moorjani is an award winning and critically acclaimed Scottish-Indian, non-binary and neurodiverse writer, storyteller and theatre maker based between  Edinburgh and London. They have perfomed all over the world, from Delhi to New York to old York. They create for both children and adults, dealing with themes queerness, anti-colonialism, myth and legend. At the core of all their work is hope, joy and kindness.

sarah-rundle.name: Sarah Rundle
sarah-rundle.bio: Sarah Rundle is a professional storyteller based in west London. She trained in community storytelling in South Cheshire, and in acting at Drama Studio London. Over the last nineeen years, Sarah has performed at a diverse range of venues, from museums and theatres, to pubs, cafés, and a Saxon longhouse.

janet-crouch.name: Janet Crouch
janet-crouch.bio:
Janet has been telling stories from the 1970's, promoting story for adults in a variety of organisations such as luncheon clubs, dementia groups, the U3A, the WI, and folk clubs.



She was privileged to tell stories at the First International Storytelling Festival in Marrakech with tellers from the UK, Morocco, Canada, USA, Finland, Ireland, and Colombia too.



This event is intended for ages 16 and older.

sarah-mooney.name: Sarah Mooney
sarah-mooney.bio:
Stories that transform, ceremonies that reconnect, wisdom that remembers 



 For twenty-five years, Sarah has been walking a ceremonial path — unwinding old tales so their original wisdom can flow through again, creating spaces where stories become medicine.



 Sarah trained with Clarissa Pinkola Estés (author of Women Who Run With The Wolves) and actors from the legendary Peter Brook Company, learning that the deepest stories aren't just told — they are lived, embodied, remembered. For the last fifteen years, She has supported initiation rites and vision quests, working with and learning from shamans in Mexico and Ecuador, discovering how the old ways of storytelling can guide transformation.

dave-tonge.name: Dave Tonge
dave-tonge.bio: Professional storyteller, author & historian. He tells at museums, heritage sites, festivals, fairs and schools. Also known as The Yarnsmith of Norwich.

sharon-jacksties.name: Sharon Jacksties
sharon-jacksties.bio:
Sharon Jacksties tells stories to audiences of adults, young people and families.



 Her dynamic and versatile performances draw on stories from all over the world.



 Interpreting the wisdom of traditional material for contemporary audiences, her repertoire includes tales that have rarely been heard in this country.

aaron-oliver.name: Aaron Oliver
aaron-oliver.bio: Prop Maker and Storyteller based in London, passionate about creating stories.

sally-pomme-clayton.name: Sally Pomme Clayton
sally-pomme-clayton.bio:
'One of the country's most highly regarded storytellers...she awakens the imagination of the audience.'  The Independent



'Virtuoso storyteller Sally Pomme Clayton has that rare gift of being able to transport the listener to other worlds, by completely inhabiting those worlds herself. Her stories are authentic and full of colour, she uses language like a painter. Her telling goes right through the skin and settles deep in the marrow of the listening bones — a wondrous experience.' Venue magazine, Bristol

monty-the-anywhens.name: Monty + The AnyWhens
monty-the-anywhens.bio: Isle of Wight based storyteller (Tony 'Monty' Hirst plus folk and blues duo, THe AnyWhens (Glenn Koppany and Sue Caiun) telling combinations of songs and stories, as well as thematic fringe festival shows.

tim-ralphs.name: Tim Ralphs
tim-ralphs.bio:
Tim Ralphs is a storyteller and active exponent of narrative in art. He also works as a public engagement consultant where he focuses on connecting academic researchers with non-specialists.



His storytelling is rooted in the oral tradition: The craft of a speaker weaving language and gesture to entrance an audience and bring a tale to life.



 His stories are drawn from a rich medley of myth and folk tale, but also include live adaptations of absurd texts and wondrous reimaginings of classic fairytale material in evocative urban settings. As an ambassador and advocate for storytelling, Tim's repertoire reflects the need for the craft to appeal to today's listeners and tomorrow's tellers.

welsh-whisperers.name: Welsh Whisperers
welsh-whisperers.bio: Memeners of the Dragon Storytellers, performing in the Welsh language, bilingually or in English.

red-phoenix-the-prize-old-mummers.name: Red Phoenix | The Prize Old Mummers

katy-cawkwell.name: Katy Cawkwell
katy-cawkwell.bio:
Katy Cawkwell has been working as a professional storyteller since 1996 in venues across the UK and beyond.



 She creates contemporary, dramatic performances for adult audiences, based on traditional narratives.  Her shows have appeared in a wide range of venues: major theatres (inc. Barbican Pit Theatre, Soho Theatre, Derby Guildhall), storytelling clubs and nine international storytelling festivals (inc. a 2 week tour of India in 2016).  Katy was one of four storytellers shortlisted for the Arts Foundation Storytelling Fellowship and has featured on Radio 3 (The Verb) and Radio 4 (The Storytellers).

kersti-stabi.name: Kersti Stabi
kersti-stabi.bio: Storyteller and singer. Kersti was one of the storytellers who founded and built the Fabula storytelling company based in Stockholm. After ten rich and hugely enjoyable years on the board and on the Fabula stage she left the company, still working in collaboration with them as often as she can.

cath-edwards.name: Cath Edwards
cath-edwards.bio: Cath Edwrds is a storyteller, telling stories to adult audiences, family audiences, very young children, school-age children, and children and young people with special needs.

matt-vaughan.name: Matt Vaughan
matt-vaughan.bio: Matthew is a storyteller of 10 years experience. He has worked since 2011 in a Public Library Service in the East-Midlands and has been lucky enough to be able to develop his storytelling practice in his professional environment, performing stories and providing interactive workshops to audiences of all ages. He has worked in early years settings, primary and secondary schools and performed in clubs. He loves to perform international folk tales and wonder tales of all kinds as well as having particular interest in the stories of working folk of Britain, drawing on stories from his own coal mining heritage and beyond, and stories about nature the wild places of this world.

cath-edwards-matt-vaughan.name: Cath Edwards | Matt Vaughan
cath-edwards-matt-vaughan.bio:
Cath Edwrds is a storyteller, telling stories to adult audiences, family audiences, very young children, school-age children, and children and young people with special needs.



Matthew is a storyteller of 10 years experience. He has worked since 2011 in a Public Library Service in the East-Midlands and has been lucky enough to be able to develop his storytelling practice in his professional environment, performing stories and providing interactive workshops to audiences of all ages. He has worked in early years settings, primary and secondary schools and performed in clubs. He loves to perform international folk tales and wonder tales of all kinds as well as having particular interest in the stories of working folk of Britain, drawing on stories from his own coal mining heritage and beyond, and stories about nature the wild places of this world.

tony-monty-hirst.name: Tony "Monty" Hirst
tony-monty-hirst.bio: Isle of Wight based traditional storyteller, Tony 'Monty' Hirst has been a listener of tales for over thirty years and a teller for five. Coming up through the ranks of folk nights and open mics, he regularly performs his own shows, as well as telling pop up tales in teas tents at music festivals around the country. A keen collaborator, he peforms with folk and blues duo The AnyWhens, and folk punk fiddle player Sophie Kate.

amelia-ace-armande.name: Amelia Ace Armande
amelia-ace-armande.bio: Ace began telling professionally with the Brighton Storytellers, and has told at the 'Get A Word in Edgeways' Spoken Word Festival, the Oxford Storytelling Festival, Beeston Tales and the Crick Crack Club. Ace toured Austria as a storyteller with Helbling Publishing. Ace is a founder of the Ropetackle Storytellers Club and mentor for the Step Up Spoken Word initiative.

p-d-brown.name: P. D. Brown
p-d-brown.bio:
Since 1985, David Brown has been telling stories in theatres, art centres, libraries, city farms and schools, in woods and fields, at camp sites and festivals from Orkney to Land's End.



 David specialises in epics, myths and legends from the so-called “Dark Ages” or early mediaeval period including the Welsh Four Branches of the Mabinogion, the Norse mythological cycle, Beowulf, Wayland the Smith and historical tales of sixth century northern British kings, from Gwynedd to Edinburgh.



 He is keen to promote storytelling for the enjoyment of adults as well as children. Ghost and horror stories are his second favourite genre which he has collected from trips to Cornwall and Iceland as well as his native Cumbria.



David first started telling stories after hearing a cassette of Robin Williamson's tales.  He learnt a couple of the stories on the cassette, commenced learning the Norse mythological cycle and has not stopped since then. To more fully recreate the voice of the bards, David's translations of appropriate poems are interspersed in the narrative of such stories, as well as his own poems composed according to the rules of Anglo-Saxon and Norse verse. The lyre — the storyteller's instrument up until the early Middle Ages —  accompanies some of these tales.

ben-haggarty.name: Ben Haggarty
ben-haggarty.bio: Ben Haggarty is Artistic Director of the Crick Crack Club and one of the UK's most popular contemporary performance storytellers. Renowned for his playful, physical and often challenging performances, his repertoire spans Indo-European fairytales for grown-ups, Greek and Irish mythology, the epic of Gilgamesh and contemporary myths of Frankenstein and The Sandman, which won him a Fringe First award for best spoken word.

steph-brittain.name: Steph Brittain
steph-brittain.bio: Steph Brittain's love for storytelling developed whilst studying Kathak dance in Paris and Delhi and it continued to grow during her MA in Drama and Movement therapy. She trained in performance at Acting International in Paris and Drama Centre London and has performed at Royal Exchange Theatre Manchester, Arcola Theatre London and for the BBC. A new voice storyteller, Steph Brittain joined Pandvani108 in 2022 as part of their artist development programme.

mark-fraser.name: Mark Fraser
mark-fraser.bio: Mark Fraser is an itinerant storyteller, traversing the land with his original tales and weird stories. Join him for a selection of the odd, ghastly and vaguely unsettling tales from his own brain and around the world. A dark hour of devilish deeds and strange characters, just perfect for a dark winter's evening. A writer, an artist and the master of the bizarre story, Mark brings twenty years of storytelling experience to Wonder Monger at Cambridge Junction.

sheema-mukherjee.name: Sheema Mukherjee
sheema-mukherjee.bio: Sheema Mukherjee, established sitar player and composer; a regular in Transglobal Underground playing sitar and electric bass; a key member in The Imagined Village project; a formidable collaborator with internationally renowned artists from many genres.

emily-hennessey.name: Emily Hennessey
emily-hennessey.bio: Emily Hennessey is a playful and dynamic performance storyteller with a great love of Hindu mythology. She has travelled over 10,000 miles across India and Nepal by train, bus, rattling rickshaw and bicycle. Emily has toured in India, Iceland and Japan and performed at venues and festivals across Europe. She spent many months at the Kattaikkuttu School in Tamil Nadu, learning from the children who perform stories from the Mahabharata through music, dance and song.

hugh-lupton.name: Hugh Lupton
hugh-lupton.bio:
Hugh Lupton is an award-winning storyteller, performer and author. He is also a poet and lyricist. His work spans over 35 years, working on both solo projects and in partnership with other artists, writers, musicians, illustrators and performers.



He has toured both nationally and internationally and has performed at the RSC, the National Theatre and the Barbican, as well as at schools, community venues and arts centres the length and breadth of Britain. His repertoire ranges from Greek epic to Grimms Fairy Tales, from Norse and Celtic myth to East Anglian folk-tales, from the Great War to John Clare. Thematically his work often explores our place in the landscape and the deep cultural memories we carry.

suited-storytellers.name: Suited Storytellers
suited-storytellers.bio: Suited Storytellers tell gritty tales with strong female protagonists. They specialise in creating hybrid performances which fuse oral tradition storytelling with poetic interventions, dialogue and carefully crafted staging.

henry-everett.name: Henry Everett
henry-everett.bio: Henry Everett is a storyteller, telling tales in the oral storytelling tradition, passed down through the generations. He tells traditional tales inspired by our connection to the land, of spirits of place and our human relationship with the magical landscape.

hannah-sneyd.name: Hannah Sneyd
hannah-sneyd.bio: Hannah Sneyd is a storyteller and healer based in the Dartmoor wilds. Her original stories arrive on the wind and thread a canny path between the familiar and the fantastical.

henry-everett-hannah-sneyd.name: Henry Everett | Hannah Sneyd
henry-everett-hannah-sneyd.bio: Henry Everett is a storyteller, telling tales in the oral storytelling tradition, passed down through the generations. He tells traditional tales inspired by our connection to the land, of spirits of place and our human relationship with the magical landscape.

royal-tales-story-club-sutton-coldfield.name: Royal Tales story club, Sutton Coldfield

tis-tales-hsf.name: 'Tis Tales, feat. Holly Medland, Sue Bailey, Merl Fluin
tis-tales-hsf.bio:
'Tis Tales is a storytelling troupe telling tales to adult audience on the Isle of Wight [Holly Medland, Sue Bailey, Tony 'Monty' Hirst, Merl Fluin]. 



 Performing in various combinations, this performance features tales from Holly Medland, Sue Bailey and Merl Fluin.

tis-tales-hms.name: 'Tis Tales, feat. Holly Medland, Sue Bailey, Tony 'Monty' Hirst
tis-tales-hms.bio:
'Tis Tales is a storytelling troupe telling tales to adult audience on the Isle of Wight [Holly Medland, Sue Bailey, Tony 'Monty' Hirst, Merl Fluin]. 



 Performing in various combinations, this performance features tales from Holly Medland, Sue Bailey and Tony 'Monty' Hirst.

ronnie-conboy.name: Ronnie Conboy
ronnie-conboy.bio:
Storytelling has been with us since the dawn of humankind, of course. Ronnie Conboy encountered storytelling as an art some 15 years ago, and was blown away by the simplicity and power of a well told story - by its ability to connect. Inspired to started telling, she practised, collaborated, got some training, and as she have developed she has found stories in every corner of my life.



For 35 years Ronnie worked in healthcare, and in recent years has used stories and storytelling techniques in her interactions with service users and colleagues, in workshops for education, for communication skills and for wellbeing.

vanessa-woolf.name: Vanessa Woolf
vanessa-woolf.bio: Vanessa Woolf tells stories for adults and kids. Vanessa is a founder of Elephant Tales (ex Story Co Op) London's beautiful free Sunday open-mic storytelling club, and lead storyteller of Connection With Wonder, who provide storytellers, programme festivals and offer year-round training.

jessie-wild.name: Jessie Wild
jessie-wild.bio: Jessie Wild is a performance Storyteller/ Creative Educator. Runs The Tell Tales Club, Cardigan / Aberteifi.

ben-haggarty-steph-brittain.name: Ben Haggarty & Steph Brittain
ben-haggarty-steph-brittain.bio:
Ben Haggarty is Artistic Director of the Crick Crack Club and one of the UK's most popular contemporary performance storytellers. Renowned for his playful, physical and often challenging performances, his repertoire spans Indo-European fairytales for grown-ups, Greek and Irish mythology, the epic of Gilgamesh and contemporary myths of Frankenstein and The Sandman, which won him a Fringe First award for best spoken word.



Steph Brittain's love for storytelling developed whilst studying Kathak dance in Paris and Delhi and it continued to grow during her MA in Drama and Movement therapy. She trained in performance at Acting International in Paris and Drama Centre London and has performed at Royal Exchange Theatre Manchester, Arcola Theatre London and for the BBC. A new voice storyteller, Steph Brittain joined Pandvani108 in 2022 as part of their artist development programme

gillian-brownson.name: Gillian Brownson
gillian-brownson.bio: Gillian Brownson is as story artist, multi-disciplinary practitioner blending writing, storytelling, acting, singing & illustration.

gillian-brownson-dragon-storytellers.name: Gillian Brownson + Dragon Storytellers
gillian-brownson-dragon-storytellers.bio: Gillian Brownson is as story artist, multi-disciplinary practitioner blending writing, storytelling, acting, singing & illustration.

tis-tales.name: 'Tis Tales
tis-tales.bio: 'Tis Tales is a storytelling troupe (Holly Medland, Sue Bailey, Tony 'Monty' Hirst, Merl Fluin) telling tales, in various combinations, to adult audiences on the Isle of Wight.

amy-douglas.name: Amy Douglas
amy-douglas.bio:
Amy Douglas is a vibrant professional storyteller with a passion for traditional stories and riddles.



 Over the past twenty years she has delved deep into the folklore of Britain, reveling in tales of the strange and macabre; memories of magic and otherworlds; proud recollections of folk history; stories to make you smile, sigh or shiver; stories that have been moulded by the land, weather and generations of tongues wagging.

jason-buck.name: Jason Buck
jason-buck.bio: Award-winning performance storyteller, author and illustrator.

amy-douglas-jason-buck.name: Amy Douglas and Jason Buck
amy-douglas-jason-buck.bio:
Amy Douglas is a vibrant professional storyteller with a passion for traditional stories and riddles. Over the past twenty years she has delved deep into the folklore of Britain, reveling in tales of the strange and macabre; memories of magic and otherworlds; proud recollections of folk history; stories to make you smile, sigh or shiver; stories that have been moulded by the land, weather and generations of tongues wagging.



Jason Buck is an award-winning performance storyteller, author and illustrator.

ian-douglas.name: Ian Douglas
ian-douglas.bio:
Ian Douglas is a Storyteller and Theatre Maker Who works extensively across the British Isles. With over 20 years of experience delivering storytelling, performance and theatre activities for schools, arts organisations, communities and festivals across Britain, he has developed a practise approach which mixes sensitivity, Commedic energy and wit leading to a style all of his own.



 The origins of Ian's work are steeped in the traditions of street theatre which has helped him develop his unique storytelling style. His work has been described as 'truly inspirational' and draws upon a rich vein of British folk tales and world myths.

maria-buckley-whatton.name: Maria Buckley Whatton
maria-buckley-whatton.bio:
Maria Whatton is a treasured storyteller and fiction author. A winner of Outstanding Female Storyteller of Great Britain in 2013 (British Awards for Storytelling Excellence) she is renowned for her compelling, vibrant performances.



 She has a vast knowledge of traditional stories passed down by her Irish father and grandmother which she brings to life with her lucid, engaging style.

chris-richardson.name: Chris Richardson
chris-richardson.bio: Chris Richardson has been telling stories a long time — as long as he can remember. But it has only been the last 10 years or so that he have sought out audiences to share the wonder of how things are, should be or could be in our wildest dreams. He has been described as quirky, in part becuase ge can't resist fiddling with a story as it comes to him, or writing something new if it says what he wants to say. All the old stories were new once, and it's up to the storytellers of today to keep the old tales relevant and to add original ones that reflect and record the way we live today.

marion-leeper.name: Marion Leeper
marion-leeper.bio:
Marion Leeper is based in Cambridge, and is familiar visitors to museums, schools and libraries around the region. She has toured her shows for grownups to clubs and festivals around the country and internationally. She tells stories for children of all ages, and specialises in multi-sensory storytelling for the very young and for people with learning difficulties, working with the charity Bag Books.



 She is a long time member of the local group Cambridge Storytellers, and is currently involved in organising the East Anglian Storytelling Festival.

mike-payton.name: Mike Payton
mike-payton.bio:
Mike Payton has been telling tall tales all his life, and decided to turn this into a career in 2008. He was Lancastrian born, but is now based in Nottingham, drawing his tales from Britain's rich heritage and also from the four corners of the earth. His delivery is  warm and engaging, and he has a penchant for the strange and unexplainable.



 For the past 10 years, Mike has co-run the ever popular Beeston Tales with ace storyteller Tim Ralphs, a monthly storytelling club that brings the best of contemporary storytelling to an adult audience. As well as his work in schools, he has performed at museums, galleries, festivals, castles, pubs, clubs and cafes throughout the land. Early in his career he was the winner of The Black Country Story Slam, and was invited by The Society for Storytelling to perform at their annual gathering. He performed at The Wellcome Trust's Day of the Dead celebrations on a day when the gallery had its busiest ever day: He has visited over 250 schools across the UK and Europe, performing and running workshops to all ages.

mike-oconnor.name: Mike O'Connor
mike-oconnor.bio:
Accomplished on the fiddle and accordion, Mike has a wealth of songs and stories.



Mike tells spell-binding stories, many with musical accompaniment, for both adult & schools audiences. His tales vary from 5 minute fireside tales to epics such as Return to Lyonesse, Tristan & Iseult, Imravoe, the Tales of the Holy Rood, & Loki. He is one of the few in the land capable of delivering such epic tales and sagas. He is the author of the best selling 'Cornish Folk Tales' published by History Press. In 2013, with Barbara Griggs he won a British Award for Storytelling Excellence for 'Return to Lyonesse'.

shane-ibbs.name: Shane Ibbs
shane-ibbs.bio:
Shane is a modern day Oral Storyteller.



Shane first heard traditional stories as a child from his father and often repeated them to his siblings and eventually his own three children. In 1997 Shane went to Canada and while he was there, he was invitated to meet with the indidgenous Metis people and be part of  their Ceremonies and a Story Healing Circle, an immense privalage. This experience not only rekindled Shane's storytelling spark, it also provided a profound shift in his thinking as he began to use 'Applied Storytelling' as a Social Worker and then as a Counselling Therapist. His life long storytelling journey continued, with fresh insight.

marcus-pibworth.name: Marcus Pibworth
marcus-pibworth.bio: Shane is a modern day Oral Storyteller who first heard traditional stories as a child from his father, then often repeated them to his siblings and eventually his own three children. In 1997 Shane went to Canada and while he was there, he was invitated to meet with the indidgenous Metis people and be part of  their Ceremonies and a Story Healing Circle, an immense privalage. This experience not only rekindled Shane's storytelling spark, it also provided a profound shift in his thinking as he began to use 'Applied Storytelling' as a Social Worker and then as a Counselling Therapist. His life long storytelling journey continued, with fresh insight.

holly-medland.name: Holly Medland
holly-medland.bio:
Holly is a storyteller who lives on the beautiful Isle of Wight.



 She tells all sorts of stories - from favourite childhood fairytales to strange local legends - and has brought these stories to adults and children in all sorts of places including museums, pubs, bookshops, festivals, schools and forests.

sue-bailey.name: Sue Bailey
sue-bailey.bio:
Sue has been telling tales for hundreds of years (Well, that's a tall tale if ever there was one!) She lives on the Isle of Wight and has told tales sheltering from storms in caves, sandblasted by beach debris, on the top deck of buses and ferries, and in many other strange ( and not so strange) places. She has helped countless children create their own stories inspired by local treasures ( both human and artefact) and is one of the organisers of the Somewhen Storytelling Festival.



She loves passing on the story gems that have come her way, intent on keeping alive the oral tradition. Folk tales, nursery tales, tales of wonder and tales to inspire or scare the pants off you – she will share them all. Just make sure you pass them on too.



Founder of the Island Storytellers, and member of 'Tis Tales, Sue's has a story for every occasion, entertaining families, schools groups and adults alike, in workshops, on story walks, and in performance settings.

lynette-hill-stephen-hobbs-rebecca-lacha.name: Lynette Hill, Stephen Hobbs, Rebecca LaChance, Peter Boyce, 2026 Bard of Stony Stratford, Gill and Patrick Larley

debs-newbold.name: Debs Newbold
debs-newbold.bio:
Debs Newbold is an internationally acclaimed, multi-award winning storytelling artist who creates fresh, playful work that gets deep under the skin.



She is a performer, director, writer, trainer and collaborator. Playful and curious, her work takes her to theatres, festivals, old buildings and outdoor spaces. Her work is bold and honest, often very funny and known for its imaginative storytelling and ability to connect with a wide range of audiences.



With over 20 years of experience, Debs has created a body of work that explores the depth of the human condition through theatre and live performance. She is in demand all over the world. Half English, half Irish, working class in origin and Brummie to her core.

giles-abbott.name: Giles Abbott
giles-abbott.bio: Giles Abbott is an award winning storyteller and an experienced vocal coach. He tells stories for all ages at club nights, festivals and schools. He provides storytelling workshops for children to increase literacy, promote team working and to inspire imagination. For adults his workshops give confidence and to remind us how to create and tell compelling story, helping anyone to lose themselves, find themselves, in the worlds evoked by our words. He has worked with major companies such as ITV and Red Bull using vocal skills to enhance presentations and speechwriting as well as teaching voice at drama schools. He is also available for voiceover work.

jon-mason.name: Jon Mason
jon-mason.bio:
Storyteller based in Brighton.



 Jon tells stories about times gone by and the modern day, blending fact and fantasy, from all over the world, for kids and grownups. Jon has given performances and workshops at festivals, community events, schools, spoken word nights, and storytelling clubs around the country — he creates shows and sessions to suit all needs.



 Jon is also a folklorist and cultural historian, researching stories in the context of their time, place and social meaning. He has appeared as a guest on BBC Radio, and teach up to university level on storytelling skills, folklore and more.

tuup.name: TUUP
tuup.bio:
TuuP was born in London of Guyanese parents.



 In the early 80s, Tuup was recognised by a small guyanese publishing house, run by Jessica & Eric Huntley, called Bogle L'Ouverture Press.



 Tuup joined the famous West London Storytelling Unit, run by artistic-director, Ben Haggarty and assistant-director, Georgiana Kebbel, a challenging innovative arts group looking at performance storytelling for adults.



In the mid 80s, Tuup formed a storytelling group with Flora Devi called 'Tellers of Time' (indian dance & folk tales coupled alongside african-caribbean tales & rhythms).

xanthe-gresham-knight.name: Xanthe Gresham Knight
xanthe-gresham-knight.bio:
Xanthe Gresham Knight is a writer and international performance storyteller with a wide repertoire. Author of 'Goddesses and Heroines' with Thames and Hudson, she has created a variety of shows celebrating the feminine divine including 'Cassandra' for Radio 4 (listen to the BBC podcast here) and The Online Goddess Lounge, funded by The Arts Council of England.



 Xanthe has been Storyteller in Residence for Harvard University, The Blanton Museum Texas, Psychologies Magazine, The Chelsea Physic Garden, Stoke and Staffordshire Libraries and 18 Hours Hastings. She worked as a Senior Lecturer in Drama and Storytelling for the University of East London for 17 years. As well as touring and working locally in Seaford, Sussex, Xanthe is Artistic Director of GuestHouse storytellers, Newhaven, East Sussex.

phil-okwedy.name: Phil Okwedy
phil-okwedy.bio: Before film, books, newspapers, or even writing itself, there was oral story. Phil Okwedy is a storyteller who stands in that ancient tradition. He has been telling stories in schools and libraries, pubs and clubs, theatres and festivals for 10 years. Phil tells tales that are deep and direct, playful and poignant, gleeful and grave. He tells them to entertain. He tells them to stir and awaken feelings of mutual understanding. He tells them because story is how we connect.

ceri-phillips.name: Ceri Phillips
ceri-phillips.bio:
Ceri John Phillips (Gwas Awen) is Cyfarwydd for Bro Dinefwr and Associate Storyteller with People Speak Up, an Arts Charity based in Llanelli.



Ceri has performed in a variety of settings: from conducting story walks and donning the stage at the Beyond the Border International Storytelling Festival to providing tales for school groups, from making the ladies at the WI chuckle with bawdy stories to sharing the ancient and enigmatic myths of Wales next to a bonfire.

michael-harvey.name: Michael Harvey
michael-harvey.bio:
Michal harvey tells stories, in both Welsh and English, from the world's oral traditions to all ages. His repertoire is based in Wales and the other Celtic countries but he can't resist a good story, wherever it's from.



Michael works in community settings, in schools, in theatres, festivals, libraries, village halls, pubs, museums and galleries. He sometimes works solo and sometimes in collaboration with other artists and production companies. He trains storytellers and those who want to become storytellers and trains teachers to use storytelling as a powerful tool for literacy and second language acquisition.

stacey-blythe.name: Stacey Blythe
stacey-blythe.bio: Composer, performer, and multi instrumentalist, Stacey Blythe has performed all over the UK and America.

laura-sampson.name: Laura Sampson
laura-sampson.bio: Storyteller, sound artist and promoter based in London.

jordan-price-williams.name: Jordan Price Williams
jordan-price-williams.bio: Jordan Price Williams is an award-winning traditional musician from Cwmafan, South Wales (cello, double bass).

tamar-eluned-williams.name: Tamar Eluned Williams
tamar-eluned-williams.bio: Tamar Eluned Williams is a professional storyteller working in the Welsh oral tradition. Tamar tells stories in Welsh and English, working with a wide repertoire of Celtic mythology, legends, and folktales, alongside stories from all over the world. She works with story in lots of different ways: telling them from memory in schools, festivals, museums and theatres, creating new shows for adult and family audiences, running storytelling workshops, and writing and directing for theatre.

matt-johnson.name: Matt Johnson
matt-johnson.bio:
Matt Johnson is a Midlands based Singer / songwriter who really wears his heart on his sleeve at the same times as completely captivating his audience.



His subjects range from his family and the love of his life through to his view of the world now and his opinion on the plight of the oceans and our wildlife.



He has a fantastic ear for melodyand bulids a hook into every song that has his audience singing back at him by the second chorus.



Equally at home alone on stage with his acoustic guitar and trusty onion or supported by his super tight band, The Reprobates.

maria-gillen.name: Maria Gillen
maria-gillen.bio:
Maria was the Irish Storyteller in Residence for Kerry Writers Museum from 2019 to 2022. She was the Artistic Director for the Listowel International Storytelling Festival 2020 to 2022. Maria is currently working as an archivist with Sheahan's Storytelling Cottage in Finuge — a 300 year old traditional Rambling House in Finuge, Co. Kerry.



She is a well-known popular storyteller on the storytelling circuit in Ireland and on the Cyberspace Platform. She is a well-known Bean An Tí (Irish Session Host) on the Irish Storytelling Circuit.

baden-prince.name: Baden Prince
baden-prince.bio: Baden Prince Baden is a writer, poet, storyteller and performer.

lynette-hill.name: Lynette Hill
lynette-hill.bio: Joyous creator and teller of tall tales and 2023 Bard of Stony Stratford

liz-weir.name: Liz Weir
liz-weir.bio: Liz Weir MBE is an award-winning Irish storyteller who has shared stories on five continents — from pubs, prisons, and hospital rooms to the Royal Albert Hall and New York's Grand Central Station.

lisa-schneidau.name: Lisa Schneidau
lisa-schneidau.bio:
Lisa Schneidau finds and tells stories that inspire, provoke curiosity and build stronger connections between people and nature.



 She works with the oral storytelling tradition, where traditional and modern stories are remembered, shared and passed on to others. You'll find her telling stories at events and venues all over the South West and beyond. Lisa's repertoire includes storytelling evenings for adults, shows and collaborations, schools and museums work, story walks and workshops.



Lisa has written four books on traditional folk tales published by The History Press.

roving-jewel.name: The Roving Jewel
roving-jewel.bio:
The Roving Jewel Mummers are storytellers, puppeteers and mummers from the East of Albion. Our goal is to return the wealth of British folk tales to the commons! We tell subversive, obscure and occasionally bawdy tales, using the heritage crafts of oral storytelling and puppetry. We travel around the region with our miniature theatre and marionettes - all built by us in Norwich - with a core band of mummers and journeying performers and musicians.



We are hedge bards at the beginning of our careers! We're learning our craft by doing: our collective background as artists, writers, medievalists and performers have brought us here. We want to learn from seasoned storytellers, master craftspeople and lifelong practitioners to keep the traditional crafts of puppetry and storytelling alive, accessible and folk to the bones. We are signatories of the Folk Union Charter, our practise champions those traditionally underrepresented in folk arts. We are a gender and neurodiverse group with a wide array of socio-economic backgrounds, and we want to see more people like us (and different) in the folk scene.

keith-wallis.name: Keith Wallis
keith-wallis.bio: Podcaster, host of The Piskie Trap.

elizabeth-dale.name: Elizabeth Dale
elizabeth-dale.bio:
Elizabeth Dale is a Cornish freelance writer, blogger and podcaster.



As she proudly proclaims, 'I AM NOT A HISTORIAN'. Rather, she is a writer who loves history and who aims to learn about and share the lesser-known stories from Cornish past.

daisy-black.name: Daisy Black
daisy-black.bio: Daisy Black's storytelling weaves medieval narratives together with English folk song.  Often moving, occasionally political, frequently feminist, just a little queer and regularly funny, my stories underline the relevance and vibrancy of medieval narratives for today's world.

a-spell-in-time.name: A Spell In Time
a-spell-in-time.bio: A Spell In Time is a unique British-Bulgarian storytelling company that bring Bulgaria's extraordinary traditional tales to English-speaking audiences for the first time...

pyn-stockman.name: Pyn Stockman
pyn-stockman.bio: Pyn Stockman performs folk and fairy tales, myths and legends from all around the world, telling to people of all ages in lots of different spaces, including libraries, useums, bookshops, theatres, schools, festivals, parks & woodlands. Pyn works with adults, young people and children to become storytellers through playful training and creative workshops and lead storytelling sessions to inspire performance making, creative writing and  well being.

chris-adriaanse.name: Chris Adriaanse
chris-adriaanse.bio:
Chris Adriaanse is a storyteller and spoken word artist based in Berwick-Upon-Tweed, Northumberland.



He tells stories in the oral tradition, where each telling begins afresh each time.

harp-and-carp.name: Harp and Carp
harp-and-carp.bio: Harp and Carp are the brainchild of Storyteller Daniel Serridge and Harper Neil Wood. Drawing on their interest in folk and ceilidh culture Harp and Carp share stories and music from the folk tradition, inspired by each others rhythms, pace and power, Daniel and Neil combine traditional folk tales and tunes with improvised arrangements of power and precision to create a uniquely moving and meditative experience. Miles to go before I sleep will celebrate the lore of the winter months from long journeys and cold wind burnt faces to crackling fires and rooms full of folk.

dan-broadbent.name: Dan Broadbent
dan-broadbent.bio: Blabbermouth Storytelling is the home of Dan Broadbent, storyteller and community heritage practitioner. Dan has over a decade of professional experience telling traditional stories, folktales and local legends to audiences across South West England, in settings ranging from museums and schools, village halls and pubs, to site-specific storywalks immersed in nature. He has made numerous appearances on local radio and television, discussing the history, archaeology and folklore of Somerset, and is a regular contributor to the BBC's Secret Somerset podcast. Prior to launching Blabbermouth, Dan's professional background was in archaeology and heritage management.

martin-maudsley.name: Martin Maudsley
martin-maudsley.bio:
Martin Maudsley is a professional storyteller based in Bridport and working across the UK in schools, theatres, festivals and for a range of national for organisations. His wide-ranging repertoire of stories consists of fresh re-tellings of traditional tales from local legends to magical myths, often combining music and audience interaction to bring the stories to life.



 Martin has a particular passion for 'wild stories' — stories that connect and engage with the natural world — and often tells stories outdoors in woodlands, farmland and riverside settings. He has worked extensively with Dorset AONB, the National Trust and Dorset Wildlife Trust in gathering, creating and telling stories about the rich tapestry of local landscapes in Dorset. In 2018 he produced a schools pack called Myths and Legends of the South Dorset Ridgeway.

ragged-storytelling-collective.name: Ragged Storytelling Collective
ragged-storytelling-collective.bio:
The Ragged Storytelling Collective create 'tales both ancient, contemporary, fantastical and grounded' woven with 'twisting tunes coiling like smoke, an invocation of something very ancient and yet completely current'.



Traditional myth meets urban magic.

hari-berrow.name: Hari Berrow
hari-berrow.bio: Hari Berrow is an interdisciplinary researcher, scriptwriter, and critic working at the intersection of media theory, psychology, neuroscience and creative writing. Her research explores ways of challenging stigma towards mental ill-health, attitudes towards mental illness in the wider media, presentations of mental ill-health in scripted works, how horror, magical realism and speculative fiction can be used to make abnormal psychology accessible to audiences and ethical and practical questions around creating presentations of mental disorder.

marc-tuckey.name: Marc Tuckey
marc-tuckey.bio: Leader of Isle of Wight Ghost Experience, guided walks around the Isle of Wight.

amy-jeffs.name: Amy Jeffs
amy-jeffs.bio: Dr Amy Jeffs writes and illustrates her critically acclaimed books from her home in Somerset. Her work engages with Britain's medieval and early modern storytelling cultures. She is a Senior Research Associate at Girton College, Cambridge, and a Fellow of the Society of Antiquaries.

soft-lap.name: Amy Jeffs | Gwen Burns | Natalie Brice
soft-lap.bio: Amy Jeffs, author of Storyland and Wild, artist Gwen Burns and musician Natalie Brice.

michael-dacre.name: Michael Dacre
michael-dacre.bio: Michale Dacre, professional storyteller specialising in traditional tales and legends from the West Country.

hannah-brailsford.name: Hannah Brailsford
hannah-brailsford.bio: Hannah Brailsford is an experienced performance storyteller & creative practitioner. For over 25 years I have been providing entertainment & storytelling for schools, events & special occasions.

dan-sedgwick.name: Dan Sedgwick
dan-sedgwick.bio:
Dan Sedgwick is a father of 5, a husband, a son, a professional storyteller, a hip hop enthusiast,and also in recovery from alcoholism, drug addiction and severe self-harm. Now in his 40s he is in by far the best shape he has ever been in, physically, emotionally and mentally.\n


His mission is to use my passion for storytelling to share a message of recovery and hope.

peter-stevenson.name: Peter Stevenson
peter-stevenson.bio: Peter Stevenson is a storyteller, children's writer, book illustrator, folklorist, film maker, crankie maker, artist book maker, organiser of the annual 3 day Aberystwyth Storytelling Festival and Y Mabinogi Project in collaboration with Aberystwyth Arts Centre, and the Wales Appalachia Project, along with Stories@Medina, a storytelling club that encourages collaborations with visual artists, musicians, and puppeteers. He has illustrated, compiled and written many children's books, travelled the country with storytelling shows for adults that use hundreds of illustrations, crankies, and live music, has written four books of Welsh folk tales for the History Press.

harriet-grindley.name: Harriet Grindley
harriet-grindley.bio:
Harriet Grindley’s stories connect with place, history, myth and community with tales traditional and new.  She particularly enjoys using Gaelic, songs, clarsach or concertina, found objects and image to create an immersive and inclusive environment.



Co-host of the popular series of storytelling ceilidh’s organised by the Burgh Blatherers at the Scottish Storytelling Centre, she tells in care homes, libraries, fairs and festivals.

inez-aponte.name: Inez Aponte
inez-aponte.bio: Performing traditional tales from around the world, Inez has been a performance storyteller for over 25 years.

sheeha-blackhall.name: Sheeha Blackhall
sheeha-blackhall.bio: Sheena Blackhall is a writer, illustrator, traditional ballad singer and storyteller in North East Scotland.

pauline-cordiner.name: Pauline Cordiner
pauline-cordiner.bio: Pauline is a storyteller from the fishing and farming traditions of the North East of Scotland and enjoys telling a variety of traditional Scottish tales, fairy tales, folk tales creation myths and the occasional home-grown tale of her own. Pauline can also bring traditional Scots songs and bothy ballads into her performance and can use these as well as puppets and costumes to bring further audience participation into the storytelling performance. Pauline loves to tell stories for schools, care homes, special needs groups, festivals, castles, out in the open – just anywhere there is an audience for the stories she loves!

shonaleigh.name: Shonaleigh
shonaleigh.bio:
Word weaver, tradition bearer and educator



 Shonaleigh is a dedicated and committed storyteller actively working to expand and share the Drut'syla tradition. Brought up in the Drut'syla tradition by her Bubbe (grandmother) she has a repertoire of over three thousand stories which can be adapted as appropriate for the theme and audience with whom she is working.

colin-urwin.name: Colin Urwin
colin-urwin.bio:
Colin Urwin is Seanchaí to the Chief of the Antrim McDonnells Randal, 15th Earl of Antrim at Glenarm Castle. He is also a folksinger, songwriter, storyteller, author and workshop facilitator. For over 25 years he has been the caller and singer in the award-winning 'Haste to the Wedding' ceilidh band. He serves on the committee of the Storytellers of Ireland and the Glens of Antrim Historical Society and is the Storyteller in Residence at The Courthouse, Shared Space and Creative Hub, Bushmills.



Steeped for a lifetime in the language, folklore and traditions of his beloved Glens of Antrim, Colin has long been influenced by traditional Irish and Scottish music, song and story. He is inspired by the people and places, the history and legends, and the sheer, rugged romance of the northeast coast of Ireland. From these he conjures up many of his beautiful ballads, magical rhyming monologues and delicately woven tales of wonder.



His rich and varied storytelling repertoire includes many time-honoured Irish and Scottish tales from around the Sea of Moyle, on which he always stamps his own distinctive mark. He is also well known for his humorous and sometimes poignant recitations after the long-established local County Antrim tradition.

clare-murphy.name: Clare Murphy
clare-murphy.bio:
Storytelling since 2006, Clare brings the ancient art of story firmly into the contemporary world where it belongs. 



Performing on stages around the world, Clare has told stories to audiences as varied as the All-Blacks coaches, Mission Critical Teams, scientists, schoolchildren and Irish President Mary Robinson.



Clare is sought after to teach storytelling skills to communities worldwide, including asylum seekers, social entrepreneurs, climate change scientists, firefighters and limbless veterans.

maria-credali.name: Maria Credali
maria-credali.bio:
Storyteller and artist based in Lichfield, in the Midlands of England. Maria Credell creates unique and memorable storytelling shows for adults and work as a performer, educator and workshop facilitator. She is a registered Artsmark practitioner and a trained creative mentor, specialising in trauma informed practice.



 Working orally and unscripted, she reworks ancient stories for modern audiences. Maria works with theatres, heritage organisations, festivals, schools, private and community groups. She can help bring myths and legends to life at your school, to celebrate special occasions with friends, and to provide unforgettable experiences for listeners. Previous clients include The Royal Shakespeare Company, Festival at the Edge and many national and international festivals.

lucy-lill.name: Lucy Lill
lucy-lill.bio: Performance storyteller based in London, Lucy Lill tells stories to all sorts of people in all sorts of places: theatres, arts centres, festivals, museums and schools. She create a shows on her own and also as part of Story Jam, with Alys Torrance. She also runs workshops and produces events.

sarah-liisa-wilkinson.name: Sarah-Liisa Wilkinson
sarah-liisa-wilkinson.bio:
A renowned teller of folktales, myths and wonder tales, Sarah Liisa has been delighting audiences for years with her juicy and arresting innovations on stories both familiar and obscure, drawing material from all over the world but most especially from Finnish and Nordic folklore.



 Her tales bring you beasts and gods dreaming in the deep green of the Finnish forest, girls wrestling many-headed trolls in the mountains, pure-hearted fools chasing magic apples in a land of wonder, and dancing devils causing chaos and joy.



 She has told stories to people in the UK and internationally in theatres, festivals, clubs, pubs, churches, boats, front rooms and all-night sleepovers. She regularly works and performs with performance-storytelling pioneers, The Crick Crack Club, and is part of multi-disciplined storytelling and music group, The Embers Collective.

tamar-eluned-williams-jordan-price-willi.name: Tamar Eluned Williams & Jordan Price Williams

sarah-lianne-lewis.name: Sarah Lianne Lewis
sarah-lianne-lewis.bio: Sarah Lianne Lewis is a Welsh composer crafting vivid and inventive music that bridges tradition and innovation. Working in both concert hall and stage, her music weaves unexpected sonorities - acoustic and electronic - into bold soundscapes that captivate and challenge in equal measure.

daniel-morden-hugh-lupton-sarah-lianne-l.name: Daniel Morden | Hugh Lupton | Sarah Lianne Lewis
daniel-morden-hugh-lupton-sarah-lianne-l.bio: Two of Britain's leading storytellers — Hugh Lupton and Daniel Morden — performing in combination with composer Sarah Lianne Lewis.

hugh-lupton-sarah-moody.name: Hugh Lupton | Sarah Moody
hugh-lupton-sarah-moody.bio: Master-storyteller Hugh Lupton accompanied by acclaimed cellist Sarah Moody (ex-Devil's Violin).

sarah-moody.name: Sarah Moody
sarah-moody.bio: Acclaimed cellist Sarah Moody, one time member of The Devil's Violin (wtih Daniel Morden and Oliver Wilson-Dickson (violin)).

corinne-harragin.name: Corinne Harragin
corinne-harragin.bio: Corinne Harragin is a Bristol-based Storyteller, Performance Maker and Trainer whose style draws out the physicality, humour and shared experience of storytelling, recentring marginalised voices in myth and folklore with her own brand of oral performance.

stephe-harrop.name: Stephe Harrop
stephe-harrop.bio:
Stephe Harrop is a contemporary storyteller, creating and sharing intelligent, irreverent spoken-word shows with audiences across the UK, and beyond. She also researches and writes about contemporary storytellers, as well as training and mentoring students to develop their own storytelling practices.



Stephe is fascinated by the challenge of using storytelling to help promote a positive sense of belonging, and a spirit of creative citizenship, across a range of communities and contexts.

owen-staton.name: Owen Staton
owen-staton.bio: Welsh Storyteller and Performer and host of The Time between Times Storytelling podcast. Ever since he was a small child, Owen has been fascinated by the amazing myths and legends that were born on his doorstep. Owen has performed all over the world as a Storyteller and Actor for over thirty years. He is available for storytelling bookings or podcast appearances and can design and write storytelling sessions for all ages. Owen also has experience as a stage, television, and Voice actor and is an experienced public speaker lecturer, and theatrical director.

henry-everett-and-hannah-sneyd.name: Henry Everett and Hannah Sneyd
henry-everett-and-hannah-sneyd.bio:
The days are lengthening, and green shoots are nudging their way through the soil. As winter loosens its icy grip on the world, it is time to celebrate Imbolc, the Celtic festival of early spring, whose name means 'in the belly.'



 Join us for Stirring Stories, a series of uplifting tales to stoke the fire within and rouse us from our winter slumber. Forget the to-do list, gather together and and allow your imagination to unfurl into the realm of myth and magic.



 This special event with Dartmoor storytellers Henry Everett and Hannah Sneyd promises a vivid array of stories formed through deep communion with the landscape. Blending traditional tales from around the world with original stories, games and song, we curate our events to closely match the energy of the season.



 Things to note: This is a family-friendly event, with a 20-minute interval and food and drink available from The Lounge Cafe - please see the MIC website for further details.

iris-skipworth-colin-rowan.name: Iris Skipworth & Colin Rowan
iris-skipworth-colin-rowan.bio:
A life for a story - A story for a life.



 This father and daughter duo bring to life a tale of thievery, giants, ghosts; and many a close brush with death. How much is a story worth? A reworking of a tale found across the British Isles in the 1800's, Colin and Iris bring a contemporary edge to these traditional tales with their unique style.

greg-chapman.name: Greg Chapman
greg-chapman.bio:
Greg has been a professional entertainer, comedy performer, magician and storyteller for over two decades, and has been an entertainer all his life - he was first placed on a stage at just three weeks old!



When not working on our documentaries, we can be found touring the country with our shows. Throughout 2026 our main tour consists of two shows, 'Greg Chapman's Discovery of Witchcraft' after a few years of touring 'The Non-Psychic 'Psychic' Show', 'The Legend of Houdini', and 'The Non-Psychic Seance'.

ffion-phillips-dragon-storytellers.name: Ffion Phillips + Dragon Storytellers
ffion-phillips-dragon-storytellers.bio:
ome and listen to stories about kings and queens, the Tylwyth Teg and fairies, dragons and monsters and ordinary people as well. Stories of Barmouth, Gwynedd and Wales, stories from long ago and tomorrow. The Dragon Storytellers will be joined by professional storyteller Ffion Phillips.



Ffion is a bilingual storyteller and folk musician with a passion for breathing new enchantment into old tales and reworking scraps of forgotten material into fresh stories. Her work often explores environmentalist themes and the natural world(s), with much of her repertoire rooted deeply in her native Eryri. As part of the 'Harebell Tellers' alongside Ailsa Dixon she has brought her collaborative shows 'Hear us and Hasten' and 5 star sell-out 'Aderyn/Bird' to the Edinburgh Fringe.

bernadette-russell.name: Bernadette Russell
bernadette-russell.bio: Author, performance storyteller, theatre maker, workshop facilitator, cabaret MC , playwright and tree planter. Bernadette performs her own stories as well and traditional stories everywhere- including cabarets, sheltered housing, schools, parks, gardens, forests, vintage clothes shops, launderettes, beaches, in peoples beds, once even inside a Police Box! She loves being outside and can often be found in the woods. She write stories on walls, chalk poetry onto pavements and leave magical notes inside strangers pockets

gaz-brookfield-and-the-company-of-thieve.name: Gaz Brookfield and The Company of Thieves
gaz-brookfield-and-the-company-of-thieve.bio:
West country based Gaz Brookfield is an award winning solo acoustic musician, with two number one albums to his name.



 He has spent the last fifteen years tirelessly touring all over the UK and beyond, building a strong and loyal following wherever he goes.



 Brookfield was the first independent musician to sell out Bristol's The Fleece (450 cap) and The Bierkeller (750 cap) and more recently SWX (1000 cap). 



 Gaz has an impressive ten album strong back catalogue. In the autumn of 2023, Gaz's ninth album, 'Morning Walking Club', saw him achieve his first number one in the Official UK Folk Albums Chart.



 Brookfield got his second number one album in September 2025, when 'Waiting for Wisdom' topped the Official UK Download Charts.



 Not a bad result for this fiercely independent musician, with no record label, no manager, no agent - just a guitar, a van, and a steadfastly belligerent refusal to give in.

gaz-brookfield.name: Gaz Brookfield
gaz-brookfield.bio:
West country based Gaz Brookfield is an award winning solo acoustic musician, with two number one albums to his name.



 He has spent the last fifteen years tirelessly touring all over the UK and beyond, building a strong and loyal following wherever he goes.



 Brookfield was the first independent musician to sell out Bristol's The Fleece (450 cap) and The Bierkeller (750 cap) and more recently SWX (1000 cap). 



 Gaz has an impressive ten album strong back catalogue. In the autumn of 2023, Gaz's ninth album, 'Morning Walking Club', saw him achieve his first number one in the Official UK Folk Albums Chart.



 Brookfield got his second number one album in September 2025, when 'Waiting for Wisdom' topped the Official UK Download Charts.



 Not a bad result for this fiercely independent musician, with no record label, no manager, no agent - just a guitar, a van, and a steadfastly belligerent refusal to give in.

jess-silk.name: Jess Silk
jess-silk.bio: Jess Silk is a guitarist, singer and songwriter from the Black Country. Her passionate and  melodic brand of folk/punk music gets people sitting up and listening. Armed only with an increasingly sticker-covered acoustic guitar, lyrics right from the heart and a distinctive, powerful voice that many don't expect, Jess plays to audiences up and down the UK and has shared stages with many well known names among the folk/punk scene.

nick-parker.name: Nick Parker
nick-parker.bio:
Glastonbury based Nick Parker started gigging in his early teens playing mandolin and singing in folk/rock/skiffle band 'Why?', and spent the next 10 years bouncing around on stages at hundreds of venues and festivals around the UK and Europe. In 2010 Nick embarked on a solo career and has since enjoyed over 30 tours around the UK and Europe, releasing five full-length albums along the way, many songs from which have been featured on BBC6 Music among many other radio stations.



 Although primarily a solo effort Nick often plays with his band 'Nick Parker and the False Alarms' which is a folky/rocky/country affair using guitars, fiddle, pedal steel, banjo, mandolin, cornet, accordion, keys, whistles, pipes, bass and drums, often with various members switching instruments when space permits. At a recent gig the band were described as 'A British Arcade Fire', something that Nick was very pleased about. Nick also often gigs as a duo with fiddle player Ben Wain.

jess-silk-matt-johnson.name: Jess Silk + Matt Johnson
jess-silk-matt-johnson.bio:
Jess Silk is a guitarist, singer and songwriter from the Black Country. Her passionate and  melodic brand of folk/punk music gets people sitting up and listening. Armed only with an increasingly sticker-covered acoustic guitar, lyrics right from the heart and a distinctive, powerful voice that many don't expect, Jess plays to audiences up and down the UK and has shared stages with many well known names among the folk/punk scene.



Matt Johnson is a Midlands based Singer / songwriter who really wears his heart on his sleeve at the same times as completely captivating his audience. His subjects range from his family and the love of his life through to his view of the world now and his opinion on the plight of the oceans and our wildlife. He has a fantastic ear for melodyand bulids a hook into every song that has his audience singing back at him by the second chorus.

jess-silk-joe-solo.name: Jess Silk & Joe Solo
jess-silk-joe-solo.bio:
Jess Silk



https://jesssilk.co.uk



Jess Silk is a guitarist, singer and songwriter from the Black Country. Her shouty but melodic brand of folk/punk music gets people sitting up and listening. Armed only with an increasingly sticker-covered acoustic guitar, lyrics right from the heart and a distinctive, gravelly voice that many don't expect, Jess plays to audiences up and down the UK and has shared stages with many well known names among the folk/punk scene.



Jess released her debut album, Break the Bottle, in May 2018 with a packed out show at her spiritual home of Katie Fitzgerald's in Stourbridge. Since then she's shared a number of other releases including two live EP's with tracks taken from shows at Katie's and HMV Merry Hill. 2020 saw the world shut down and Jess release two (almost completely) self produced EPs, the first of which, If We're Damned, was put together from songs that had been gathering metaphorical dust on her hard drive, and the second, There's A Bar At the End Of the World, a collection of songs written between March and August taking their inspiration from the events of the year. At the end of August 2021 Jess released her second full-length album Blitz Spirit, and in April 2025 released her new EP 'Old, Broken Isle' which was recorded at Metway Studios in Brighton.



In recent years Jess has appeared at a number of festivals including Bearded Theory, Rebellion, Beautiful Days and Glastonbury where she shared a stage with Billy Bragg as part of his Radical Roundup on the Left Field stage. In March 2023 Jess played six shows supporting the Levellers and she continues to tour around the UK playing festivals, supports and her own headline tours



Joe Solo



https://joesolomusic.com



Joe Solo is an award-winning musician, writer, poet, activist, broadcaster and washing machine engineer from Scarborough. His musical odyssey began in 1987 fronting a bash-em-out band at school, and has seen him play nine countries either as lynchpin of pop-punk upstarts Lithium Joe or hammering out his unique brand of Folk, Punk and Blues in his own right.



On top of releasing twenty one albums since 2004, Solo has written a series of children's books for Unison on social justice and equality; ran a research project on the Hull Pals Battalions in the First World War; hosts a weekly radio show; had one of his poems used in an international campaign to educate on dementia awareness; co-runs 'May Day Festival of Solidarity' an annual event bringing together voices from music, poetry and politics to celebrate International Worker's Day; and has opened for the likes of Paul Heaton & Jacqui Abbott, Billy Bragg, TV Smith, Grace Petrie, Pete Wylie, and The Blockheads; whilst his songs have been played by John Peel on Radio One, Mike Harding on Radio Two and Tom Robinson on BBC6Music.



In 2024, Joe's album 'Sledgehammer Songs' made both the Top 40 of the Official UK Folk Chart, and the Official Album Download Chart, and the resulting tour raised £12,435 for community causes.



Live, Solo has a growing reputation as both a performer and raconteur, being both thought-provoking, comical and punch-the-air political often in the same breath. He is not an artist you forget in a hurry.

sorrel-nation.name: Sorrel Nation
sorrel-nation.bio: Travelling troubadour Sorrel Nation brings a soulful mix of folk-rock to country and beyond and possesses one of those voices that makes you immediately stop what you're doing to give her your full attention. She engages audiences with emotive lyrics, 70s inspired style and a voice full of soul, which further increases the seductive power of her songs. Known for penning songs that can truly open the heart to grief and like all valued songwriters possess a giving presence...she heralds gorgeous ballads that could become Sorrel Nation's trademark sound.
